## Assistant (2026-04-28 14:09:06)

I’ll compress the branches into a single decision architecture and separate what is now structural, what is finite-audit, and what remains genuinely open.

_Thought for 7s_

No nomenclature changes.

The current rank-two branch has now consolidated into one clean architecture:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{local finite exactness}
+
\textbf{plain-wall analytic coercivity}
+
\textbf{finite-model transfer to O2/O3}.
}
\]

The central correction remains:

\[
\boxed{
\text{D4 gives }H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),\text{ not automatically }H(D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G).
}
\]

So the honest downstream route is:

\[
B_Y=0
\Rightarrow
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}.
\]

The jet family remains an analytic auxiliary or a future derivative-channel route, not the default D4 wall. 

# 1. Master consolidation

Everything now reduces to two finite-control packages.

## A. Local exactness package

Construct bounded live covectors:

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

such that they kill actual/local/nuisance directions and normalize the D4 live pair:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

Then:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0
\]

implies

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

This replaces the bulkier matrix statement

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0,
\qquad
L_{2Y}^*(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\gg Q^{-A}.
\]

The bounded dual rows prove both exactness and live-pair survival.

## B. Analytic finite Riesz package

D4 gives the plain wall:

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

RankTwoCoercivity needs:

\[
\Gamma_G\in H^2,\qquad \Gamma_{G,2}\in L^2.
\]

So we need:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}:
\quad
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}.
\]

This is a finite Riesz/Markov statement for

\[
\mathcal W_I
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

because

\[
\Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3\in\mathcal W_I.
\]

With a basis \(\psi_i\), this is just:

\[
G_2\preceq Q^C G_0,
\]

where

\[
G_0=(\langle\psi_i,\psi_j\rangle_{L^2(I^*)}),
\qquad
G_2=(\langle\psi_i,\psi_j\rangle_{H^2(I)}).
\]

So both local algebra and analytic closure are finite Gram/Riesz problems.

# 2. The \(s_7\)-row: no actual septic package scalar

The clean theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\sigma_7^{\nabla,\can}=0.
\]

D4 must be transverse:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This gives:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\qquad
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

The important hidden connection is that this is the same phenomenon as the older pre-trivialization warnings: raw coefficient drift is unreliable. The \(s_7\)-coordinate must be covariant in the moving quotient

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]

So the \(s_7\)-row is not “no order-seven Taylor motion.” It is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{no transverse covariant package motion.}
}
\]

# 3. The \(s_9^Y\)-row: slot exactness, not brute-force bilinear algebra

The \(Y12\)-row is now a Schur cascade.

Best case:

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}} W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

Then fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variations cannot supply the primitive \(Y12\) same-point whitening slot.

If this fails, do not immediately abandon rank two. Continue:

\[
\text{source slot 1}
\to
\text{source slot 2}
\to
\text{coupled bilinear image}.
\]

Only the final coupled failure creates an actual live \(Y12\)-column.

The useful abstraction is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{SlotExactness}(\mathcal S,\mathcal D,\mathcal A,c_{\live})
}
\]

with:

\[
\mathcal A\subseteq\mathcal D,
\qquad
c_{\live}\notin_{Q^{-A}}\mathcal D.
\]

This same lemma covers the whitening slot, both source slots, and the coupled image.

# 4. \(E_2/R_{\lab}\): reduced to “no live type + atom-local fit”

The no-atom-fitting sector has stopped being broad. It is now exactly:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

The only forbidden structure is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{live type}+\text{atom-local fit}.
}
\]

The three concrete guardrails are:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
\]

If these hold, \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) does not contribute to \(B_Y\). Fixed/coherent/residual remnants are routed elsewhere.

This is a major simplification: \(E_2\) and labelled mismatch are not local blockers unless the formalism allows atom-fitted live-fiber counterterms.

# 5. The corrected rank-two wall route

Once \(B_Y=0\), the D4 matrix survives:

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

Thus:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Then the analytic closure is:

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\text{ small}
\]

\[
\overset{\mathrm{LocalMarkov}}{\Longrightarrow}
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\text{ small}
\]

\[
\overset{\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}}{\Longrightarrow}
\|q''\|_{L^2(I)}
\text{ small}
\]

contradicting Gate–1.

The key identity is still:

\[
E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2,
\]

with

\[
E_1=q''-(6q-2q^3),
\qquad
E_2=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

# 6. Weighted transfer is the next hidden gap to make explicit

The agents now have local wall mass. But O2/O3 needs weighted smooth mass:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G|^2\,d\nu.
\]

So we need a named transfer lemma:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Input:

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Output, on selected good blocks:

\[
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)
\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

This requires:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},\qquad N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}
\]

or a block selection where those weights are controlled. This should be pulled into the next prompt set because otherwise the bridge into O2/O3 remains implicit.

# 7. O2/O3 remains scalar

Once the weighted plain-wall mass is known:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A},
\]

choose at the smooth aggregate stage:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

Then run scalar O2/O3 with:

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

No vector O2/O3 and no jet O2/O3 are needed.

# 8. Finite coherent amplitude fallback is explicit but secondary

If coherent amplitude remains after \(B_Y=0\), it must be fixed/block-coherent, not atom-local.

If vector amplitude Schur fails, it forces:

\[
q''=6q-2q^3+\Phi_1,
\]

\[
q(q')^2=\Phi_3.
\]

The division-free compatibility residual is:

\[
\mathcal P_{\mathrm{pair}}
=
q^3(\Phi_3')^2
-
\Phi_3
\left(
\Phi_3+2q^2(6q-2q^3+\Phi_1)
\right)^2.
\]

So amplitude failure is a finite forced-pair model, not a return to the rank-one moving ODE trap.

Still, this is not the main line. The main line should be:

\[
B_Y=0
+
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}.
\]

# 9. The deepest consolidation: one finite-control principle

The whole branch can now be described by one meta-principle:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteModelRieszControl}.
}
\]

It has several instances:

\[
\text{bounded dual rows }(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)
\]

for local exactness;

\[
G_2\preceq Q^C G_0
\]

for \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\);

bounded leverage for coherent amplitude spaces;

finite-model nonabsorption for O2/O3;

forced-pair compatibility if amplitude Schur fails.

So the rank-two route is no longer an ad hoc pile of estimates. It is a finite-model control program.

# 10. Final consolidated route

The best current route is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}
+
Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha}
+
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
+
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesDualRows}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
\Rightarrow
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\Rightarrow
\text{plain wall mass}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
\Rightarrow
\text{weighted wall mass for O2/O3}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\text{scalar O2/O3 with }G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
}
\]

# Bottom line

The rank-two branch is now highly consolidated.

The main local target is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{construct bounded dual rows }(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\text{ killing actual/local data and detecting }(e_7,e_Y).
}
\]

The main analytic target is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
}
\]

for the finite algebra

\[
\mathcal W_I=D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

The main correction is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{do not use jet derivatives as D4 wall components without derivative D4 channels.}
}
\]

And the main missing bridge to make explicit is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

  lambda_min(L_2Y^*(I-P_D2Y)L_2Y) >= Q^{-A}.

  ell_9^Y(A_2Y)=0.

     J_{7,9}/D_{2Y}.

     lambda_min(L_2Y^*(I-P_D2Y)L_2Y) >= Q^{-A}.

        ell_9^Y(I_can^(2))=0.

     DW[eta]E_2.

Deliverable:

# Agent 5 — analytic wall closure: LocalMarkov + RankTwoCoercivity

```text
You are Agent 5.

Your task:
Prove or falsify the corrected analytic rank-two wall closure.

Important correction:
  D4 supplies only the plain wall:

    {Gamma_G, Gamma_{G,2}}.

  Do not use D Gamma_G or D^2 Gamma_G as D4 wall components unless DerivativeD4Channels_2Y is separately proved.

Correct route:

  plain D4 wall
  + LocalMarkov_GammaG
  + RankTwoCoercivity
  + Gate1.

Targets:

  LocalMarkov_GammaG:

    ||Gamma_G||_{H^2(I)}
      <= Q^C ||Gamma_G||_{L^2(I^*)}.

  RankTwoCoercivity:

    ||Gamma_G||_{H^2(I)}
    + ||Gamma_{G,2}||_{L^2(I)}
    small
      =>
    ||q''||_{L^2(I)} small.

Tasks:

1. Verify normalization:

     Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3.

   Define:

     f(q)=6q-2q^3,
     E_1=q''-f(q),
     E_2=q^(4)-f'(q)q''.

   Then:

     Gamma_G=-6E_1,
     Gamma_{G,2}=E_2.

2. Re-derive identity:

     E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2.

3. Prove exact flatness:

     E_1=E_2=0
       =>
     q in {0,sqrt(3),-sqrt(3)} locally,
       =>
     q''=0.

4. State quantitative RankTwoCoercivity:

     ||q''||_{L^2(I)}
       <= Q^C(
          ||Gamma_G||_{H^2(I)}
          + ||Gamma_{G,2}||_{L^2(I)}
        )^theta.

5. Prove/reduce LocalMarkov_GammaG.

   Let V_I be the local finite EF/AFE chart for q.

   Define:

     W_I = D^2 V_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I.

   Since Gamma_G in W_I, prove:

     ||w||_{H^2(I)}
       <= Q^C ||w||_{L^2(I^*)}

   for all w in W_I.

6. Express LocalMarkov as finite Gram/Riesz condition.

   With basis psi_i of W_I:

     G_0=(<psi_i,psi_j>_{L^2(I^*)}),
     G_2=(<psi_i,psi_j>_{H^2(I)}).

   Need:

     G_2 <= Q^C G_0.

7. Include residual-compatible version:

   If q=q_X+r, prove:

     dist_{H^2(I)}(Gamma_G(q),W_I) <= Q^{-B}.

8. Gate--1 closure:

   Show:

     small plain wall
       =>
     small q''
       contradicts Gate--1.

Deliverable:
  A PlainWallRankTwoClosure theorem, including LocalMarkov_GammaG and RankTwoCoercivity obligations.
```

# Agent 6 — weighted transfer and scalar O2/O3 for the plain wall

```text
You are Agent 6.

Your task:
Bridge the corrected plain rank-two wall lower into O2/O3.

Important correction:
  O2/O3 should use the honest D4 wall family:

    G_wall^{(2)} = {Gamma_G, Gamma_{G,2}}.

  Do not use the jet family unless derivative D4 channels are separately proved.

Targets:

  WeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y,

  and scalar O2/O3 for

    G_* in {Gamma_G, Gamma_{G,2}}.

Tasks:

1. Start from the analytic wall lower supplied by Agent 5:

     ||Gamma_G||_{L^2(I^*)}
     + ||Gamma_{G,2}||_{L^2(I)}
       >= Q^{-A}.

2. Prove or reduce weighted transfer:

     int N_sm |H|^2(
       |Gamma_G|^2+|Gamma_{G,2}|^2
     ) dnu
       >= Q^{-A'}.

   State needed hypotheses:

     |H| >= Q^{-A} on selected good blocks;
     N_sm >= Q^{-A} or controlled slow variation;
     block selection preserves Gate--1 mass;
     I and I^* weights are compatible.

3. If weights are not uniformly bounded below, give a block-selection lemma:

   Select subblocks where

     N_sm |H|^2

   has polynomial lower average while retaining polynomial wall mass.

4. Finite-family scalar selection:

   From:

     int N_sm |H|^2(
       |Gamma_G|^2+|Gamma_{G,2}|^2
     ) dnu >= Q^{-A},

   choose one fixed component at smooth aggregate stage:

     G_* = Gamma_G
     or
     G_* = Gamma_{G,2}.

   Do not select G_* atomwise after extraction.

5. Scalar O2/O3 theorem:

   Run O2/O3 with:

     W_* = N_sm |H|^2 |G_*|^2.

   Bandwidth:

     d_0^{(2)}
       =
     d_N + 2d_H + 2 max(d_GammaG,d_GammaG2).

   Verify:

     d_0^{(2)} <= d_max

   or state the corrected d_max.

6. If finite coherent amplitude exists:

   Do not put projected wall into O2/O3 unless projection complexity is controlled.

   Use raw plain wall for O2/O3, then apply vector amplitude Schur afterward.

7. If vector amplitude Schur fails, route to forced pair branch:

     q''=6q-2q^3+Phi_1,
     q(q')^2=Phi_3,

   with compatibility residual:

     P_pair =
       q^3(Phi_3')^2
       -
       Phi_3(
         Phi_3+2q^2(6q-2q^3+Phi_1)
       )^2.

8. Optional fallback:

   If WeightedPlainWallTransfer fails because LocalMarkov requires derivatives,
   do NOT use jet wall directly.
   Instead propose either:
     DerivativeD4Channels_2Y,
     or block-difference derivative propagation.

Deliverable:
  A WeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y theorem and scalar O2/O3 integration memo.
```

_Thought for 15s_

Yes — I’m sure about the correction as a **logical guardrail**.

The D4 output currently established under \(B_Y=0\) is:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix},
\]

so, after coefficient conditioning,

\[
|F_{2Y}|^2
\asymp
|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
\]

That directly gives the **plain vector wall**

\[
\boxed{\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.}
\]

It does **not** by itself give

\[
D\Gamma_G,\qquad D^2\Gamma_G
\]

as D4-forbidden channels. The agent batch explicitly corrected this: the jet family is analytically convenient, but it is not automatically supplied by D4 unless we prove a derivative-channel theorem or block-difference propagation theorem. 

The corrected logic is:

\[
B_Y=0
\Rightarrow
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

then, to use the coercivity identity requiring \(H^2\)-control of \(\Gamma_G\), we need:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}:
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
Q^C\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2}
\text{ small}
\]

implies, via LocalMarkov,

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2}
\text{ small},
\]

and then RankTwoCoercivity gives

\[
\|q''\|_{L^2}\text{ small},
\]

contradicting Gate–1.

So the corrected downstream stack is:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
+
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}.
}
\]

The jet wall can be restored only if one of these is proved:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{DerivativeD4Channels}_{2Y}:
D4\text{ genuinely supplies }HD\Gamma_G,\ HD^2\Gamma_G.
}
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\text{block-difference propagation: neighboring legitimate D4 observations control }D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G.
}
\]

Without one of those, using

\[
\{\Gamma_G,D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

inside D4/O2O3 would be smuggling analytic derivatives into the forbidden lower.

So: yes, the correction is right. The only nuance is that the jet family remains useful as an **analytic auxiliary** or as a **future wall** after a separate derivative-channel theorem.

# 1. Main local consolidation: \(B_Y=0\) is a two-row certificate

The best local statement is no longer the raw matrix certificate. It is the bounded-row form:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0
}
\]

with

\[

\]

and

\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

\[

and the live-pair Schur lower. So the proof should target explicit bounded row covectors, not separately prove a Gram lower.

The two rows are:

\[
\ell_7(u)=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}
{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}

and

\[

\]

# 2. The \(s_7\)-row is now exact: line-contained actuals, transverse D4

The \(s_7\)-row has two finite checks:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7
\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
\]

The key hidden refinement is the basepoint/tangent distinction.

A nonzero canonical septic determinant is not automatically a \(B_Y\)-column. It must be classified as:

\[
\text{zero},
\quad
\text{fixed/coherent offset},
\quad
\text{actual/local tangent}.
\]

For \(B_Y=0\), the necessary condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

The stronger and cleaner sufficient condition is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

That avoids recentering and amplitude complications. If only a fixed/coherent septic offset remains, rank two is not locally dead, but the offset must be routed to basepoint recentering or \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\).

# 3. The \(Y12\)-row is a whitening naturality theorem

The \(s_9^Y\)-row is now controlled by the live whitening quotient:

\[
\mathcal Q_W^Y=W/W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

The clean certificate is:

\[
\boxed{
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
}

\[
\boxed{
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
}

and, after Agent 3’s refinement,

P_{D_W^\perp}\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]=0

}
\]

where

\[
\mathfrak W_Y
=
DW_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WDW_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}.
\]

This is a strong consolidation. The \(Y12\)-row is not an open-ended \(D^2N_0\) audit. It is first a **whitening naturality** audit:

\[

\text{fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variations transport whitening; they do not recompute the primitive same-point slot.}
}
\]

Only if this fails should agents descend to source-slot or coupled-bilinear tests.

\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.

\]

\[

-

\[

\text{fixed/coherent/residual } \neq B_Y;
\quad
\text{atom-local live fit } = B_Y.
}
\]

This means \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) should not be treated as a local rank obstruction unless the formalism explicitly introduces local live-package counterterms.

# 5. New connection: \(Y12\)-row and \(R_{\lab}\) share the same whitening quotient

The same defect

\[
\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]

appears both in:

and in:

\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}.

\mathcal Q_W^Y.

\mathrm{WhiteningNaturality}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha,+}
}
\]

\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

and

\[

\]

That consolidates the \(Y12\)-row and labelled-transition analysis.

The agents correctly preserve:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal G_{\wall}^{(2)}=\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
}
\]

D4 does not supply:

\[
D\Gamma_G,\quad D^2\Gamma_G.
\]

So the rank-two analytic closure is:

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2}

plus

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
\]

to get the \(H^2\) control needed by RankTwoCoercivity.

\boxed{
\mathrm{PlainWallRankTwoClosure}.
}
\]

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G},
\]

\[
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity},
\]

\[
\mathrm{Gate1}.
\]

Conclusion:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

# 7. \(H\) must be routed as part of D4 coefficient-goodness

Agent 6 found another important hidden dependency.

The wall is really:

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

If \(H\) is small on the wall-mass region, D4 gives no useful lower. The best consolidation is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

This should include:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

The preferred treatment is to classify \(H\):

## Case A — \(H\) is a coefficient/normalization denominator

Then include \(|H|\ge Q^{-A}\) in D4 chart-goodness and prove chart routing preserves polynomial Gate–1 mass.

## Case B — \(H\) is finite low-complexity amplitude

Then prove \(H\)-wall compatibility / sublevel control.

## Case C — \(H\) is locally adjustable

Then this D4 normalization is not valid for rank-two lower; use another chart or carrier.

This is a real structural dependency. It should be in all future rank-two prompts.

# 8. Weighted transfer needs an \(R^{-C}\)-scale lower set

Another nontrivial correction: for Remez transfer, a \(Q^{-C}\)-measure good set is too small if the finite algebra has degree \(R\sim \log Q/\log\log Q\). We need:

\[
\boxed{
|E_\Omega|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
}
\]

where

\[
E_\Omega=\{N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

Then finite-algebra Remez gives polynomial loss.

So the weighted transfer theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}.
}
\]

Inputs:

\[
\int_I W_2\ge Q^{-A}|I|,
\qquad
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]

\[
|E_\Omega|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and vector Remez:

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
\Rightarrow
\int_E W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

Then:

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}|I|.
\]

This is the right bridge to O2/O3.

# 9. O2/O3 remains scalar, but only after correct routing

After weighted transfer:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A},
\]

select

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

at the smooth aggregate stage, not atomwise.

Then run scalar O2/O3 with:

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

Bandwidth:

\[
d_0^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
\]

No \(\rho_\Gamma\), no moving line, no jet derivative components.

# 10. Final consolidated dependency graph

The rank-two route now has this exact dependency graph:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}
}
\]

from:

\[
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}
+
\mathrm{WhiteningNaturality}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha,+}
+
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
+
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}.
\]

This gives:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

gives:

\[
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
}
\]

on good mass.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

gives unweighted plain wall mass.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}
}
\]

gives weighted smooth wall mass.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\text{scalar O2/O3 with }G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
}
\]

# 11. Best “go further” goodie

The major new goodie is that \(H\)-compatibility should not be a late analytic estimate if \(H\) is a coordinate denominator. It should be absorbed into:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

This is cleaner and safer. It prevents trying to prove weighted transfer on charts where the D4 output is already degenerate.

So the next agent prompts should probably include:

1. identify \(H\) and classify it as denominator/amplitude/local;
2. prove D4CoeffGoodRouting;
3. prove \(R^{-C}\)-scale WeightGoodSet for \(N_{\sm}|H|^2\);
4. prove vector Remez for \(W_2\);
5. continue bounded dual-row local certificate;
6. finish LocalMarkov / RankTwoCoercivity.

# Bottom line

The rank-two branch is now more precise than before, but also has two added routing conditions:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}.
}
\]

The correction remains confirmed:

\[
\boxed{
\text{plain wall only, unless derivative D4 channels are proved.}
}
\]

The deepest consolidation is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank two is a bounded-row local certificate plus coefficient-good routed plain-wall coercivity.}
}
\]

So the minimal local condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

The stronger preferred condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})=0
\text{ as a section.}
}
\]

If only a fixed/coherent offset remains, rank two is not locally dead; it creates recentering or vector amplitude bookkeeping. That said, the cleanest route is still to prove the stronger zero-section statement.

# 4. \(Y12\)-row and labelled transition should be merged into one whitening-naturality theorem

The \(Y12\)-row is controlled by:

\[
\mathcal Q_W^Y=W/W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

The row certificate is:

\[
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

But Agent 3 adds the crucial transition defect:

\[
\mathfrak W_Y
=
DW_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WDW_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}.
\]

We also need:

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]=0
\quad\text{locally}.
\]

This means the \(Y12\)-row and part of \(R_{\lab}\) are the same problem. They should be consolidated as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WhiteningNaturality}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha,+}.
}
\]

This theorem should output both:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

and

\[
D_{\loc}\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})=0.
\]

That avoids duplicating the same whitening quotient audit in two places.

# 5. \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) has only three outcomes

The no-atom-fitting sector is now clean enough to reduce to a trichotomy:

\[
\boxed{
\text{local fitting}}
\quad|\quad
\boxed{
\text{finite coherent amplitude}}
\quad|\quad
\boxed{
\text{residual}.
}
\]

There is no fourth category.

If

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0,
\]

then it changes \(B_Y\), with routing:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\Rightarrow
\text{rank-one fallback},
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1)
\Rightarrow
\text{vertical }Y12\text{ death},
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2
\Rightarrow
\text{live-fiber absorption}.
\]

If it is fixed/coherent, it belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

If it is residual, it belongs to completion.

This is a useful paper-level simplification:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ affects }B_Y\text{ only through atom-local live-fiber fitting.}
}
\]

# 6. A hidden ordering rule: local exactness must precede amplitude projection

Agent 4’s last point is important.

We cannot project \(F_{2Y}\) away from \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) and then feed that projected object back into \(B_Y\). That would mix global amplitude fitting with local tangent structure.

The correct order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{first prove }B_Y=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{then handle }\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ by vector amplitude Schur.}
}
\]

This also applies to the wall: O2/O3 should use the raw plain wall, not a projected wall, unless projection branch complexity is separately controlled.

# 7. \(H\) is now a routing issue, not a late weighted-transfer nuisance

This is the newest structural insight.

The prefactor \(H\) should be classified before wall transfer. There are three cases:

\[
\boxed{
\text{A. coefficient/normalization denominator}}
\]

Then include

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

inside

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}.
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{B. finite low-complexity amplitude}}
\]

Then prove \(H\)-wall sublevel/nonabsorption.

\[
\boxed{
\text{C. locally adjustable amplitude}}
\]

Then this D4 normalization fails unless we renormalize or use another chart/carrier.

The best route is to make \(H\)-goodness part of chart routing:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

This theorem should guarantee that a polynomial portion of Gate–1/wall mass lies where:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

and

\[
c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9
\]

are polynomially conditioned.

# 8. Weighted transfer needs an \(R^{-C}\)-large good set

The \(Q^{-C}\)-measure good set was identified as too weak. If the wall lies in a degree-\(R\) finite algebra, Remez losses scale like:

\[
\left(\frac{C}{|E|/|I|}\right)^R.
\]

So we need:

\[
\boxed{
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
}
\]

not merely

\[
Q^{-C}|I|.
\]

The correct transfer theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}.
}
\]

Inputs:

\[
\int_I W_2\ge Q^{-A}|I|,
\]

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]

\[
E_\Omega=\{N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}\},
\qquad
|E_\Omega|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and vector Remez:

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
\Rightarrow
\int_E W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

Then:

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}|I|.
\]

# 9. LocalMarkov is finite Riesz control for \(\mathcal W_I\)

The honest plain wall still requires:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}:
\quad
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}.
\]

This is finite Riesz control for:

\[
\mathcal W_I
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

Because:

\[
\Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3
\in\mathcal W_I.
\]

With basis \(\psi_i\):

\[
G_2\preceq Q^C G_0.
\]

This connects directly to the weighted transfer Remez issue. Both require the same finite-atlas regularity package.

# 10. One more hidden unification: finite model control appears four times

A single meta-theorem should likely cover:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteModelRieszControl}.
}
\]

It appears as:

1. bounded dual rows for \(B_Y=0\);
2. \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\);
3. vector Remez for \(W_2\);
4. bounded leverage for \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\);
5. O2/O3 finite \(R_{\fold}\)-model separation.

This is a major unification. The rank-two branch is increasingly a finite-model/Riesz problem rather than a bespoke analytic wall problem.

# 11. Final dependency graph after this pass

The clean dependency graph is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{SepticLineExactness}_{\can}
+
\mathrm{C7Transversality}
+
\mathrm{WhiteningNaturality}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha,+}
+
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
+
\mathrm{NuisanceRowDeadness}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\mathrm{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
}
\]

on routed good mass.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\text{choose }G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\text{ at smooth aggregate stage}
}
\]

and run scalar O2/O3.

# 12. What should be elevated as “new goodies”

The most important goodies to carry forward are:

\[
\boxed{
\text{bounded dual rows replace live Gram audit.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{\(s_7\)-row is covariant line exactness, not raw septic coefficient vanishing.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{\(Y12\)-row and labelled whitening transition share one quotient theorem.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{\(E_2/R_{\lab}\) is local only if it is atom-fitted live type.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{\(H\)-compatibility belongs in D4 chart routing.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{weighted transfer needs \(R^{-C}\)-large good sets.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{plain wall remains honest; jet wall needs derivative-D4 channels.}
}
\]

# Bottom line

The rank-two route is now best described as:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{bounded-row local exactness}
}
\]

plus

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{D4 coefficient-good routed plain-wall coercivity}
}
\]

plus

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{\(R^{-C}\)-scale weighted transfer into scalar O2/O3.}
}
\]

The correction is stable. The new hidden insight is that the downstream \(H\) and \(N_{\sm}\) issues are not afterthoughts; they are routing/finite-atlas conditions that must be stated before O2/O3.

   quantitatively:

     |Delta_7^nabla(C_7)| >= Q^{-A}.

6. Distinguish basepoint and tangent:

   But for B_Y=0, actual derivative columns must satisfy:

     Delta_7^nabla(I_can^(2))=0.

# Agent 5 — PlainWallRankTwoClosure: LocalMarkov + RankTwoCoercivity + Gate–1

```text id="e7ycr1"
You are Agent 5.

Your task:
Prove or falsify the corrected analytic closure for the honest plain rank-two wall.

Critical correction:
  D4 gives only the plain wall {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.
  Do NOT use D Gamma_G or D^2 Gamma_G as D4 wall components unless DerivativeD4Channels_2Y is separately proved.

Target theorem:

  PlainWallRankTwoClosure.

Assume:

  B_Y=0,

  D4CoeffGoodRouting gives coefficient conditioning,

  LocalMarkov_GammaG,

  RankTwoCoercivity,

  Gate1.

Conclude:

  ||Gamma_G||_{L^2(I^*)}
  + ||Gamma_{G,2}||_{L^2(I)}
    >= Q^{-A}.

Tasks:

1. Verify normalization:

     Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3.

   Define:

     f(q)=6q-2q^3,
     E_1=q''-f(q),
     E_2=q^(4)-f'(q)q''.

   Verify:

     Gamma_G=-6E_1,
     Gamma_{G,2}=E_2.

2. Derive:

     E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2.

3. Prove exact flatness:

     E_1=E_2=0
       =>
     q in {0,sqrt(3),-sqrt(3)} locally
       =>
     q''=0.

4. State quantitative RankTwoCoercivity:

     ||q''||_{L^2(I)}
       <= Q^C(
          ||Gamma_G||_{H^2(I)}
          + ||Gamma_{G,2}||_{L^2(I)}
        )^theta.

5. Prove/reduce LocalMarkov_GammaG.

   Let V_I be local finite EF/AFE chart for q.

   Define:

     W_I = D^2 V_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I.

   Since Gamma_G in W_I, need:

     ||w||_{H^2(I)}
       <= Q^C ||w||_{L^2(I^*)}

   for all w in W_I.

6. State finite Gram form:

     G_0=(<psi_i,psi_j>_{L^2(I^*)}),
     G_2=(<psi_i,psi_j>_{H^2(I)}),

   need:

     G_2 <= Q^C G_0.

7. Include residual-compatible version:

     dist_{H^2(I)}(Gamma_G(q),W_I)<=Q^{-B}.

8. Gate--1 closure:

   Show small plain wall implies small q'', contradicting Gate--1.

Deliverable:
  A PlainWallRankTwoClosure theorem with exact LocalMarkov and RankTwoCoercivity dependencies.
```

1. Classify H:

   A. coefficient/normalization denominator;
   B. finite low-complexity amplitude;
   C. locally adjustable/free amplitude.

2. Preferred route:

   If H is a denominator, include:

     |H| >= Q^{-A}

   in D4CoeffGood_2Y alongside:

     |c_7|, |c_7^{-1}|,
     |alpha_9|, |alpha_9^{-1}|,
     |beta_9| <= Q^A.

3. Prove/reduce D4CoeffGoodRouting_2Y:

   A polynomial portion of Gate--1/wall mass lies on D4-good charts.

   If not, route H-degenerate branch:
     alternate D4 chart;
     renormalization;
     H-sublevel finite model;
     or no-go.

4. Weighted transfer:

   Let:

     W_2=|Gamma_G|^2+|Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
     Omega=N_sm |H|^2.

   Need:

     int W_2 >= Q^{-A}

   implies:

     int Omega W_2 >= Q^{-A'}.

5. Correct Remez-scale condition:

   Need good-weight set:

     E_Omega={Omega >= Q^{-A}}

   with:

     |E_Omega cap I| >= R^{-C}|I|,

   not merely Q^{-C}|I|.

6. Prove/reduce vector wall Remez:

   If E subset I and |E|>=R^{-C}|I|, then:

     int_E W_2 >= Q^{-C} int_I W_2.

7. State WeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y^R:

   Inputs:
     unweighted wall mass;
     R^{-C}-large Omega-good set;
     vector wall Remez.

   Output:
     weighted smooth wall mass.

8. Scalar component selection:

   From weighted mass for W_2, choose fixed:

     G_* in {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}

   at smooth aggregate stage, not atomwise.

9. Scalar O2/O3:

   Run with:

     N_sm |H|^2 |G_*|^2.

   Bandwidth:

     d_0^{(2)}
       =
     d_N+2d_H+2 max(d_GammaG,d_GammaG2).

Deliverable:
  A D4CoeffGoodRouting_2Y + WeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y^R theorem package, with failure branches.
```

# 1. Main consolidation: \(B_Y=0\) is now a row-level theorem with five blockers

The local theorem is now very clean. Define two bounded rows:

\[
\ell_7(u)=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)},
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(u)=\omega_Y(P_Wu)

or, if needed, a triangularized version of \(\ell_9^Y\). Then \(B_Y=0\) follows once five row-level blockers vanish:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_WD_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

Together with

\[
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A},
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})\ge Q^{-A}|a_Y|,
\]

these give bounded dual rows, hence live-pair survival and

\[
\boxed{B_Y=0.}

This is now the best compact local certificate.

# 2. New local goodie: triangularization makes the two-row certificate more flexible

Agent 2 surfaced a useful row-basis flexibility.

Even if a primitive \(Y12\) covector \(\omega_Y\) sees some \(e_7\)-leakage,

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=\kappa,

we can set

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7.
\]

Then:

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_7)=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

This only changes the lower-left D4 coefficient:

\[
\beta_9\mapsto \widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\kappa c_7,
\]

and does not change the determinant condition

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

So exact \(\omega_Y(e_7)=0\) is not necessary. What is needed is

\[
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A,
\]

plus the \(s_7\)-row exactness. This makes the bounded-dual-row theorem more robust.

The one thing that **is** dangerous is the opposite leakage:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0.
\]

That would mean the order-nine primitive carrier has an order-seven quotient shadow, suggesting a bad two-grade decomposition or quotient overcollapse.

# 3. \(s_7\)-row: actual versus formal is now decisive

The \(s_7\)-row is controlled by:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla.

+

6q_X^2r''

+

with polynomial \(C^4\)-bounds for \(q_X\).

\[
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

Then Remez gives:

\[
\int_{E_I}W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2,
\]

so:

\[
\int_I\Omega_{\sm}W_2\ge Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2.
\]

This avoids circular weighted transfer.

# 10. \(H\)-routing and finite atlas should be ordered before O2/O3

The stable route is:

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
\]

first, with:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,|c_7^{-1}|,|\alpha_9|,|\alpha_9^{-1}|,|\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

Then:

\[
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\]

gives plain wall mass.

Then:

\[
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}
\]

gives the O2/O3 input.

This order prevents \(H\)-smallness from appearing late as a mysterious failure.

# 11. Best current proof skeleton

The rank-two proof skeleton should now be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RowExactness}_{2Y}
}
\]

where five blockers vanish.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

so:

\[
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

so:

\[
\boxed{
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}
}
\]

so:

\[
\boxed{
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

Then select:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

at the smooth aggregate stage and run scalar O2/O3.

# 12. Best next prompts should target these exact gaps

The next round should probably assign agents to:

1. **NuisancePreservesRows**: grade-homogeneous row-dead nuisance, live-pair survival.
2. **ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates**: close \(T_{\lower}^{\arith}\) for the \(s_7\)-row.

4. **EFCompletion\(_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\)**: prove order-four wall residual completion.
5. **FiniteAtlasRiesz\(_{2Y}\)**: ProductRiesz, \(D^4\)-regularity, Remez, Markov.
6. **D4CoeffGoodRouting + WeightedTransfer**: \(H\)-classification, predetermined \(R^{-C}\)-good sets, scalar O2/O3.

# Bottom line

The hidden consolidation is:

\[
\boxed{

}
\]

The main local leftovers are:

and

_Thought for 7s_

Read. No nomenclature changes.

This batch makes the rank-two route more precise and reduces several remaining questions to reusable modules. The best consolidation is now:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Rank two = row-normal-form local exactness + core-routed plain-wall transfer.}
}
\]

The correction is still stable:

\[
\boxed{
\text{D4 supplies only }H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}
\]

No \(D\Gamma_G\) or \(D^2\Gamma_G\) wall is available unless a separate derivative-D4-channel theorem is proved. 

# 1. Local algebra is now in final audit form

The local \(B_Y=0\) theorem is now:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LiveFiberAvoidance}_{2Y}^{\mathrm{row}}.
}
\]

It has five row conditions:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_WD_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

With live survival:

\[
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})\ge Q^{-A}|a_Y|,
\]

and polynomial row bounds, this gives:

\[
\boxed{B_Y=0.}
\]

This should be the canonical local theorem statement going forward.

# 2. Row triangularization is now mandatory bookkeeping

The row pair should be placed in normal form before rank classification.

Start with:

\[
\ell_7=\Delta_7^\nabla/\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7),
\]

and primitive row \(\omega_Y\). If

\[
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

define:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7.
}
\]

Then:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

provided:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

This row normal form prevents false graph slopes. Raw slopes shift by \(\kappa\):

\[
\rho_Y=\rho_{\raw}-\kappa.
\]

The D4 lower-left coefficient must also be updated:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
}
\]

So the D4 matrix is:

\[
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Rank two still depends on:

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

But D4 coefficient-good routing must use \(\beta_9^{\tri}\), not the raw \(\beta_9\).

# 3. Important subtlety: \(\omega_Y(e_7)\) is harmless; \(\ell_7(e_Y)\) is not

A bounded nonzero

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)
\]

is safe because triangularization removes it.

But:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0
\]

is structural danger. It means the primitive order-nine \(Y12\) carrier has an order-seven septic shadow. That indicates grade leakage, quotient overcollapse, or a defective \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) row.

So future row audits should not waste effort proving \(\omega_Y(e_7)=0\). They should prove:

\[
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A
\]

and focus hard on:

\[
\boxed{\ell_7(e_Y)=0.}
\]

# 4. \(s_7\)-row has become package-functor factorization

Agent 2’s best consolidation is that \(s_7\)-exactness can be proved by a factorization theorem:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\big|_{\mathcal M_{\can}}
=
\chi_{\can}\,Q_5^{\pkg}\big|_{\mathcal M_{\can}}.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This implies:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

The actual lower audit is the same theorem applied to actual lower directions:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

The decisive guardrail remains:

\[
\boxed{
\text{formal }C_7\text{ carriers are not actual lower-source tangents.}
}
\]

A formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection matters only if there is an actual one-parameter source family with:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\dot q(0))\neq0.
\]

# 5. \(Y12\)-row is now reusable TypedSlotNoLocality

The \(Y12\)-row has compressed to:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TypedSlotNoLocality}.
}
\]

For the current row:

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

\[
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(R_{\lab}^Y+E_2^Y)=0.
\]

This gives:

\[
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Together with triangularization, we only need \(\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0\), not initial orthogonality to \(e_7\).

# 6. \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) is sealed unless extraction order is violated

Agent 4 has now sealed this sector with three rules:

\[
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+},
\]

\[
\mathrm{NoFeedbackFromAmplitude}_{2Y},
\]

\[
\mathrm{PreExtractionSeparation}_{2Y}.
\]

The result is:

\[
\boxed{
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
}
\]

So:

\[
B_Y=\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})
\]

provided no atom-local live fitting or post-extraction feedback is allowed.

The key operational guardrail:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Coherent amplitude spaces must be fixed before hard extraction.}
}
\]

They cannot be added to \(D_{2Y}\), used to define \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\), or used to choose the D4 chart/local quotient.

# 7. Analytic side: order-four completion is now flexible but still real

The previous theorem:

\[
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
\]

can be weakened to:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Instead of proving directly:

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4}\le Q^{-B},
\]

it suffices to complete the actual wall variables:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2}(\Gamma_G,\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2}(\Gamma_{G,2},\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This is enough because RankTwoCoercivity applies to the **actual** wall variables. The finite approximants only supply LocalMarkov/Remez transfer.

Full \(H^4(q)\)-completion remains a sufficient route, but not necessary.

A more source-natural sufficient theorem is:

\[
q''\text{-completion in }H^2
\]

plus affine reconstruction control for \(q\).

# 8. FiniteAtlasRiesz is now the central analytic theorem

The analytic finite model is:

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^4\mathcal V_I+D^2\mathcal V_I+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

Or, in \(p=q''\)-language:

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot \mathcal P_I.
\]

\[
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
\]

should supply:

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
\]

via finite approximants, and Remez for \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) on predetermined \(R^{-C}\)-large sets.

So LocalMarkov and weighted transfer are one theorem, not two independent analytic worlds.

# 9. Weighted transfer is best stated through predetermined good cores

Agent 6’s latest consolidation is strong.

For each routed block \(I\), define a predetermined core:

\[
E_I^{\core}\subset I
\]

before wall extraction. It may depend on:

\[
H,\quad c_7,\quad \alpha_9,\quad \beta_9^{\tri},\quad N_{\sm},\quad \text{chart data},
\]

but not on:

\[
\Gamma_G,\quad \Gamma_{G,2},\quad W_2,\quad \text{extracted atoms}.
\]

Require:

\[
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and on that core:

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A},
\]

with D4 coefficients good.

Then Remez gives:

\[
\int_{E_I^{\core}}W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2,
\]

where:

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

Thus:

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2.
\]

This removes the last circularity in the transfer layer.

The theorem should be named:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

# 10. D4CoeffGoodRouting should become D4CoeffCoreRouting

The old statement “a polynomial portion of wall mass lies on D4-good charts” is slightly late because it references wall mass.

The cleaner non-circular statement is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

For every selected block, there is a predetermined core of \(R^{-C}\)-measure where:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9^{\tri}|\le Q^A,
\]

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then Remez transfers any wall mass into that core.

This is cleaner than selecting a good set after seeing \(W_2\).

# 11. Current final dependency graph

The rank-two route is now:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RowExactness}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\text{ on predetermined good cores.}
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

Then choose:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

at the smooth aggregate stage and run scalar O2/O3.

# 12. Highest-value next attacks

The next round should hit:

1. **D4CoeffCoreRouting**: build predetermined \(R^{-C}\)-large cores with \(H,c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9^{\tri},N_{\sm}\) good.
2. **WallVariableCompletion**: direct completion of \(\Gamma_G\) in \(H^2\), \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) in \(L^2\), avoiding unnecessary full \(H^4(q)\) where possible.
3. **FiniteAtlasRiesz**: product/Remez/Markov for \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).
4. **NuisancePreservesRows**: still under-audited locally.
5. **ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates**: actual versus formal \(C_7\)-motion.
6. **RowNormalForm propagation**: ensure all \(\rho_Y,\rho_\Gamma,\beta_9\) fallback formulas use \(\beta_9^{\tri}\).

# Bottom line

The hidden consolidation is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{The local side is essentially row-normal-form exactness.}
}
\]

The hidden analytic opportunity is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Replace full }H^4(q)\textbf{ completion by direct wall-variable completion.}
}
\]

The hidden transfer opportunity is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Use predetermined coefficient/smooth-good cores, then Remez transfers the wall mass.}
}
\]

This is the cleanest rank-two architecture so far.

_Thought for 9s_

No nomenclature changes.

Looking again, the hidden consolidation is that the rank-two branch now has **three normal forms** that should be made canonical:

\boxed{
\text{row normal form}}
\quad+\quad
\boxed{
\text{wall-variable finite-atlas normal form}}
\quad+\quad
\boxed{
\text{predetermined-core transfer normal form}}.
\]

These three normal forms remove most ambiguity from the route. The correction remains: D4 gives only

\[
\boxed{H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})}
\]

as the honest wall. No jet wall is available unless separately proved. 

# 1. Main hidden consolidation: rank two is now basis-normalized

The local row pair should always be put into row normal form before deciding the branch.

Start with:

\[
\ell_7=\Delta_7^\nabla/\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7),
\]

and a primitive \(Y12\)-row \(\omega_Y\). If

\[
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

then define:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7.
}
\]

Then the live basis is normalized:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

This means every branch decision must use the **triangularized** coordinates, not raw \((\ell_7,\omega_Y)\)-coordinates.

The D4 coefficient must also be triangularized:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
}
\]

So the D4 matrix is:

\[
\boxed{
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
}
\]

The determinant is still:

\[
c_7\alpha_9.
\]

So row normal form preserves rank two but fixes slope bookkeeping.

# 2. Hidden opportunity: rank-one fallback must be rewritten in row-normal form

If rank two fails but the actual/local image is graph-like,

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

in the triangular basis, then the correct rank-one wall ratio is:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
=
\frac{\beta_9-(\kappa+\rho_Y)c_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

So any old rank-one formula

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

is valid only if the row was already normalized with

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=0.
\]

This is a serious bookkeeping fix. It should be propagated into every rank-one fallback prompt and note.

# 3. \(Y12\)-row exactness got easier

Because of triangularization, the primitive \(Y12\)-row no longer needs to prove

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=0.
\]

It only needs:

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\qquad
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A,
\]

and

\[
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

This relaxes the whitening audit.

The dangerous leakage is instead:

\[
\boxed{\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0.}
\]

That would mean the primitive order-nine \(Y12\) carrier has an order-seven septic shadow. That is a quotient/grade failure, not a harmless row-leakage issue.

So the local priorities should be adjusted:

\[
\boxed{
\text{prove }\ell_7(e_Y)=0\text{ hard;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{only bound }\omega_Y(e_7)\text{ polynomially.}
}
\]

# 4. \(s_7\)-row: package-functor factorization is the cleanest proof

The \(s_7\)-row should now be attacked as:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\big|_{\mathcal M_{\can}}
=
\chi_{\can}Q_5^{\pkg}\big|_{\mathcal M_{\can}}.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

This is stronger and cleaner than checking derivatives one-by-one. If it holds, then:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

The same logic handles actual lower directions:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

The important guardrail remains:

\[
\boxed{
\text{formal }C_7\text{ carrier}\neq\text{actual lower tangent}.
}
\]

A formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection matters only after actual \(s_7\)-motion is exhibited.

# 5. \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) should stay sealed

The Agent 4 module is now stable enough to stop reopening unless there is a concrete counterexample.

The sealed statement is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
+
\mathrm{NoFeedbackFromAmplitude}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{PreExtractionSeparation}_{2Y}
\Rightarrow
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
}
\]

This means:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ is not a }B_Y\text{ source unless atom-local live-fiber fitting or post-extraction feedback exists.}
}
\]

So future local work should focus on:

\[
\boxed{
T_{\lower}^{\arith},\quad \mathcal N_{\sing},\quad \ell_7(e_Y).
}
\]

Not on re-expanding \(E_2/R_{\lab}\).

# 6. Analytic consolidation: complete wall variables, not necessarily \(q\) in \(H^4\)

The earlier sufficient condition was:

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

That is clean but may be too strong. The more flexible theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Require directly:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2}(\Gamma_G,\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B},
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2}(\Gamma_{G,2},\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is enough because RankTwoCoercivity is applied to the **actual** wall variables. The finite approximants are only used to transfer \(L^2\)-smallness into \(H^2\)-smallness via LocalMarkov/Riesz.

Full \(H^4(q)\)-completion remains sufficient, but no longer necessary.

This is a major analytic opportunity.

# 7. \(q''\)-source formulation is probably the right completion language

If the canonical source is naturally

\[
p=q'',
\]

then the wall spaces can be written as:

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

where \(\mathcal P_I\) models \(p=q''\), and affine modes are included or normalized.

Then:

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

This is more natural than a raw \(q\)-chart with \(D^4\mathcal V_I\), because it places the two extra derivatives exactly on \(p=q''\).

The completion target becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

plus affine reconstruction control for \(q\).

This should be the analytic framing going forward.

# 8. FiniteAtlasRiesz now has a tighter formulation

The finite-atlas theorem should cover:

\[
\mathcal W_G
\]

and

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

It must supply:

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
\]

for the finite approximant, and Remez on predetermined \(R^{-C}\)-large sets for both wall components.

The key product spaces are:

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot \mathcal P_I.
\]

So the analytic obstruction is now:

\[
\boxed{
\text{ProductRiesz/Leverage for fixed-degree wall algebras.}
}
\]

Not rank-one wall finite type.

# 9. Weighted transfer is now a core theorem

The transfer layer should be phrased as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

For every selected block \(I\), define a predetermined core:

\[
E_I^{\core}\subset I
\]

before wall extraction. It may depend on:

\[
H,\ c_7,\ \alpha_9,\ \beta_9^{\tri},\ N_{\sm},\ \text{chart data},
\]

but not on:

\[
\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2},W_2,\text{extracted atoms}.
\]

Require:

\[
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and on this core:

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A},
\]

with D4 coefficients conditioned.

Then finite-atlas Remez gives:

\[
\int_{E_I^{\core}}W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2,
\]

so:

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2.
\]

This removes circularity from weighted transfer.

# 10. D4CoeffGoodRouting should become core routing

The better theorem is not:

\[
\text{wall mass lies on D4-good charts}.
\]

That refers to wall mass and is too late.

The better theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

It should construct \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined cores where:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9^{\tri}|\le Q^A,
\]

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then Remez moves wall mass onto the core.

This is the cleanest non-circular transfer architecture.

# 11. Current condensed proof architecture

The route is now:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RowExactness}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\text{ on predetermined cores.}
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

is selected at the smooth aggregate stage, and scalar O2/O3 runs with:

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

# 12. Best next hidden-opportunity attacks

The next prompts should focus less on broad exploration and more on these precise modules:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

Build \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined cores using \(H,c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9^{\tri},N_{\sm}\).

\[
\boxed{
2.\ \mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}
}
\]

Complete \(\Gamma_G\) in \(H^2\), \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) in \(L^2\), ideally from \(q''\)-source \(H^2\)-completion plus affine reconstruction.

\[
\boxed{
3.\ \mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

Prove ProductRiesz/Remez/Markov for \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}
}
\]

Audit row-dead nuisance and no \(e_Y-\lambda e_7\) collapse.

\[
\boxed{
5.\ \mathrm{ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates}
}
\]

Close the actual lower \(s_7\)-row.

\[
\boxed{
6.\ \mathrm{RowNormalFormPropagation}
}
\]

Propagate \(\beta_9^{\tri}\), \(\rho_Y^{\tri}\), and \(\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}\) into rank-one fallback notes.

# Bottom line

The hidden opportunity is that the proof has become much less ad hoc:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Local side: row normal form + five row blockers.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Analytic side: wall-variable completion + finite-atlas Riesz.}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Transfer side: predetermined coefficient/smooth-good cores + Remez.}
}
\]

The strongest new simplification is replacing full \(H^4(q)\)-completion with direct wall-variable completion, while keeping the plain-wall correction intact.

Target theorem:

  RowNormalFormPropagation_2Y.

Definitions:

  ell_7 = Delta_7^nabla / Delta_7^nabla(C_7),

  omega_Y = primitive Y12 row,

  kappa = omega_Y(e_7),

  ell_9^Y = omega_Y - kappa ell_7,

  beta_9^tri = beta_9 - kappa c_7.

Critical correction:
  Row normal form changes beta_9 to beta_9^tri.
  It does not create derivative wall channels.
  D4 wall remains the plain pair {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.

Tasks:

1. State the row-normal-form assumptions:

     ell_7(e_7)=1,
     ell_7(e_Y)=0,
     omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
     |omega_Y(e_7)| <= Q^A.

2. Prove triangularization:

     ell_9^Y = omega_Y - omega_Y(e_7) ell_7

   satisfies:

     (ell_7,ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
     (ell_7,ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),

   with polynomial row norm.

3. Prove exactness preservation:

   If:

     ell_7(A_2Y)=0,
     omega_Y(A_2Y)=0,

   then:

     ell_9^Y(A_2Y)=0.

   Likewise for D_loc(E_2+R_lab) and N_sing.

4. Update D4 matrix:

     M_D4^tri =
       [[c_7,0],
        [beta_9^tri,alpha_9]],

   where:

     beta_9^tri = beta_9 - kappa c_7.

   Prove determinant remains:

     det M_D4^tri = c_7 alpha_9.

5. Update rank-two coefficient-good routing:

   D4CoeffGood_2Y must use:

     |H| >= Q^{-A},
     |c_7|, |c_7^{-1}|,
     |alpha_9|, |alpha_9^{-1}|,
     |beta_9^tri| <= Q^A.

6. Update rank-one fallback ratio.

   If triangularized:

     B_Y = C(1,rho_Y),

   then forbidden scalar wall is:

     H alpha_9( Gamma_{G,2} + rho_Gamma^tri Gamma_G ),

   with:

     rho_Gamma^tri =
       (beta_9^tri - rho_Y c_7)/alpha_9.

   Explain that the old formula using beta_9 is valid only if kappa=0.

7. Prove branch classification must be done after triangularization:

   raw slope rho_raw converts by:

     rho_Y = rho_raw - kappa.

8. Failure routing:

   - kappa super-polynomial:
       row normal form ill-conditioned;

   - ell_7(e_Y) != 0:
       grade leakage / quotient overcollapse;

   - beta_9^tri super-polynomial:
       D4 coefficient-good routing failure;

   - alpha_9=0:
       D4 not rank two.

Deliverable:
  RowNormalFormPropagation_2Y theorem and updated rank-two/rank-one coefficient formulas.
```

# Agent 2 — ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates and \(s_7\)-factorization

```text id="ag2-lower"
You are Agent 2.

Your task:
Close or break the actual lower component of the s_7-row.

Target theorem:

  ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates.

Preferred stronger framing:

  SepticPackageFunctorFactorization:

    Q_7^pkg,nabla|_{M_can}
      =
    chi_can Q_5^pkg|_{M_can},

  hence:

    Q_7^pkg,nabla M_can subset C A_5^f.

Need in particular:

  Delta_7^nabla(I_can^(2)+T_lower^arith)=0.

Critical guardrail:
  Formal C_7 carriers and formal C_7 -> Y12 connections are NOT actual lower-source motion.
  They enter B_Y only if actualized by a real actual-source/nuisance/local tangent.

Critical correction:
  This is local algebra only. D4 wall after B_Y=0 is still the plain wall {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.

Tasks:

1. Define the moving quotient:

     f_m / C A_5^f(m),

   and the covariant determinant:

     Delta_7^nabla(u)=omega_m(Q_7^pkg,nabla u,A_5^f).

2. Prove or falsify package-functor factorization:

     Q_7^pkg,nabla|_{M_can}
       =
     chi_can Q_5^pkg|_{M_can}.

   Equivalently:

     omega_m(Q_7^pkg,nabla q,A_5^f)=0

   for every canonical actual source section q.

3. Derive derivative consequences:

   If the section identity holds, prove:

     Delta_7^nabla(I_can^(2))=0

   for first and second fixed-target actual variations.

4. Define T_lower^arith strictly.

   Include:
     actual EF/gamma/chart/tail lower deformations;
     actual normalization/representative lower freedoms;
     genuine nuisance lower directions if part of actual model.

   Exclude:
     formal D4 carrier variables;
     formal C_7 labels;
     formal C_7 -> Y12 connections without actual tangent;
     proof-thickening parameters;
     free corrected-package coordinates not present in actual source.

5. Actualization test.

   A formal C_7 carrier is dangerous only if there exists an actual one-parameter family q_can(t) with:

     Delta_7^nabla(d/dt|_0 q_can(t)) != 0.

   Search for or rule out such a family.

6. Finite lower matrix audit:

     U_lower^actual = columns of actual lower directions,

     M_lower,7 = Q_7^pkg,nabla U_lower^actual.

   Need:

     rank[A_5^f, M_lower,7]=1.

7. D4 contrast:

   Need:

     rank[A_5^f,Q_7^pkg C_7]=2,

   with polynomial separation.

8. Failure routing:

   - actual transverse septic tangent:
       classify its triangularized fiber vector;

   - graph C(1,rho_Y):
       rank-one fallback;

   - vertical/second slope/full rank:
       current Y12 route dies;

   - formal-only transverse carrier:
       ignore for B_Y unless actualized;

   - covariant determinant unavailable:
       repair Q_7^pkg/trivialization.

Deliverable:
  ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates theorem or explicit actual lower package-coordinate counterexample.
```

# Agent 3 — NuisancePreservesRows\(_{2Y}\)

```text id="ag3-nuis"
You are Agent 3.

Your task:
Prove or falsify nuisance row-deadness and live-pair preservation.

Target theorem:

  NuisancePreservesRows_2Y.

Need:

  Delta_7^nabla(N_sing)=0,

  omega_Y(P_W N_sing)=0,

while preserving live rows:

  Delta_7^nabla(C_7) != 0,

  a_Y notin W_dead^fixed,

and preserving two-grade independence:

  e_Y - lambda e_7 notin D_2Y + N_sing

with polynomial angle.

Critical correction:
  Nuisance audit is local algebra only.
  D4 wall after B_Y=0 is the plain wall {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.

Tasks:

1. Define N_sing by type.

   Separate:
     chart/center/scale representatives;
     gauge/normalization;
     tail/truncation residual representatives;
     wrong-origin order-nine artifacts;
     transported formed-source artifacts;
     true local nuisance;
     forbidden live package coordinates.

2. Prove s_7-row nuisance deadness:

     Q_7^pkg N_sing subset C A_5^f,

   equivalently:

     Delta_7^nabla(N_sing)=0.

   Also prove C_7 remains transverse modulo nuisance:

     dist(Q_7^pkg C_7, C A_5^f + Q_7^pkg N_sing) >= Q^{-A}|C_7|.

3. Prove s_9^Y-row nuisance deadness:

     P_W N_sing subset W_dead^fixed,

   equivalently:

     omega_Y(P_W N_sing)=0.

   Also prove e_Y remains live:

     dist(a_Y, W_dead^fixed + P_W N_sing) >= Q^{-A}|a_Y|.

4. Audit two-grade homogeneity.

   Need:

     N_sing = N_7 direct-sum N_9

   or block-triangular with no live 7<->9 relation.

   Rule out:

     e_Y - lambda e_7 in D_2Y + N_sing.

5. Include row-normal-form interaction.

   Prove nuisance row-deadness survives triangularization:

     ell_9^Y = omega_Y - omega_Y(e_7) ell_7.

6. Finite matrix tests:

   s_7:
     rank[A_5^f, Q_7^pkg N_sing]=1,
     rank[A_5^f, Q_7^pkg C_7]=2 modulo nuisance.

   s_9:
     P_W N_sing subset W_dead^fixed,
     dist(a_Y,W_dead^fixed+P_W N_sing)>=Q^{-A}|a_Y|.

7. Failure routing:

   - nuisance has transverse s_7:
       genuine gauge kills first row, or quotient overcollapse;

   - nuisance has primitive Y12:
       vertical death, graph collapse, or quotient overcollapse;

   - nuisance identifies e_Y with e_7:
       rank-two collapse;

   - row norm blowup:
       algebraic quotient but no analytic conditioning.

Deliverable:
  NuisancePreservesRows_2Y theorem or precise quotient-overcollapse no-go.
```

# Agent 4 — WallVariableCompletion\(_{2Y}\)

```text id="ag4-wallcomp"
You are Agent 4.

Your task:
Replace overly strong full H^4(q)-completion with direct wall-variable completion where possible.

Target theorem:

  WallVariableCompletion_2Y.

Need:

  dist_{H^2}(Gamma_G, W_G) <= Q^{-B},

  dist_{L^2}(Gamma_{G,2}, W_G2) <= Q^{-B}.

This is sufficient for the corrected plain-wall route because RankTwoCoercivity applies to the actual wall variables; finite approximants are only used for LocalMarkov/Remez.

Critical correction:
  D4 supplies only the plain wall {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.
  This theorem supplies analytic approximation/control, not derivative D4 channels.

Tasks:

1. State the strong sufficient route:

   If q=q_X+r and:

     ||r||_{H^4(I)} <= Q^{-B},

   with q_X polynomially C^4-bounded, then:

     ||Gamma_G(q)-Gamma_G(q_X)||_{H^2}
     +
     ||Gamma_{G,2}(q)-Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)||_{L^2}
     <= Q^{-B'}.

   Verify expansions:

     R_G =
       -6r''
       +(36-36q_X^2)r
       -36q_X r^2
       -12r^3.

     R_G2 =
       r^(4)-6r''
       +6q_X^2 r''
       +12q_X r q_X''
       +12q_X r r''
       +6r^2 q_X''
       +6r^2 r''.

2. Formulate weaker direct wall completion:

     There exist finite wall spaces W_G,W_G2 such that:

       dist_{H^2}(Gamma_G(q),W_G)<=Q^{-B},

       dist_{L^2}(Gamma_{G,2}(q),W_G2)<=Q^{-B}.

   Explain why this is enough:
     LocalMarkov applies to finite approximant of Gamma_G;
     RankTwoCoercivity applies to actual Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}.

3. Source-variable formulation.

   Let p=q'' and finite source space P_I approximate p.

   Define:

     V_I = A_1 + I^2 P_I

   with affine modes included or normalized.

   Wall spaces:

     W_G = P_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I,

     W_G2 = D^2 P_I + P_I + Sym^2 V_I * P_I.

   State source completion:

     ||p-p_X||_{H^2(I^*)} <= Q^{-B}

   plus affine reconstruction control implies wall-variable completion.

4. Affine reconstruction.

   Prove or reduce:

     (q-q_X)''=p-p_X,

   with affine modes matched/included, gives:

     dist_{H^4(I)}(q-q_X,A_1) <= Q^C ||p-p_X||_{H^2(I^*)}.

5. Gamma/background audit.

   If p=q'' contains B_2/gamma/background terms, require exact inclusion or H^2-level completion at p-level:

     B_2, B_2', B_2''

   or scale-correct equivalents.

6. Failure routing:

   - Gamma_{G,2} not finite-completable in L^2:
       rank-two analytic closure stalls;

   - Gamma_G finite-completable only in L^2, not H^2:
       LocalMarkov bridge fails;

   - affine reconstruction uncontrolled:
       Gamma_G can drift despite p-completion;

   - wall spaces incompatible with actual q normalization:
       RankTwoCoercivity cannot be applied.

Deliverable:
  WallVariableCompletion_2Y theorem, with strong H^4 route and weaker direct wall-variable route.
```

# Agent 5 — FiniteAtlasRiesz\(_{2Y}\) in \(p=q''\) form

```text id="ag5-riesz"
You are Agent 5.

Your task:
Prove or reduce the finite-atlas Riesz/Remez theorem in the source-variable formulation.

Target theorem:

  FiniteAtlasRiesz_2Y.

Use p=q'' when useful.

Finite source space:

  P_I models p=q''.

Reconstructed q-space:

  V_I = A_1 + I^2 P_I,

with affine modes included or normalized.

Wall spaces:

  W_G = P_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I,

  W_G2 = D^2 P_I + P_I + Sym^2 V_I * P_I.

Critical correction:
  D4 supplies only {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.
  Derivatives are controlled analytically by this theorem; they are not D4 wall components.

Tasks:

1. Define P_I and V_I.

   Include:
     dimension <= R^C;
     basis;
     denominator bounds;
     I subset I^* with R^{-C} buffer;
     derivative control for P_I through D^2;
     affine mode handling.

2. Define wall spaces:

     W_G = P_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I,

     W_G2 = D^2 P_I + P_I + Sym^2 V_I * P_I.

3. Prove Markov for W_G:

     ||w||_{H^2(I)} <= Q^C ||w||_{L^2(I^*)}

   for all w in W_G.

   Finite Gram form:

     G_2 <= Q^C G_0.

4. Prove Remez for W_G and W_G2.

   For predetermined E subset I with:

     |E| >= R^{-C}|I|,

   prove:

     int_E |w|^2 >= Q^{-C} int_I |w|^2.

5. Prove vector wall Remez.

   For:

     W_2=|Gamma_G|^2+|Gamma_{G,2}|^2,

   prove:

     int_E W_2 >= Q^{-C} int_I W_2

   by scalar Remez for whichever component carries half the mass.

6. ProductRiesz subtheorems.

   Need polynomial conditioning for:

     Sym^3 V_I,

     Sym^2 V_I * P_I.

   Identify product leverage risks and how to quotient near-relations.

7. Relate to WallVariableCompletion.

   FiniteAtlasRiesz applies to the finite approximants supplied by WallVariableCompletion_2Y, not directly to uncontrolled actual walls.

8. Failure routing:

   - product leverage spike:
       finite-atlas degeneracy / multiscale refinement;

   - no D^2 control of P_I:
       Gamma_{G,2} uncontrolled;

   - affine modes ill-conditioned:
       reconstruction failure;

   - denominators small:
       chart routing failure;

   - I^* crosses chart boundary:
       Markov/Remez failure;

   - product dimension too large:
       refine atlas.

Deliverable:
  FiniteAtlasRiesz_2Y theorem in p=q'' form and sub-obligation checklist.
```

# Agent 6 — D4CoeffCoreRouting + CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer

```text id="ag6-core"
You are Agent 6.

Your task:
Finalize the non-circular transfer layer using predetermined coefficient/smooth-good cores.

Targets:

  D4CoeffCoreRouting_2Y,

  CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y.

Critical correction:
  D4 wall is only {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.
  No jet wall unless derivative-D4 channels are separately proved.

Definitions:

  W_2 = |Gamma_G|^2 + |Gamma_{G,2}|^2.

  beta_9^tri = beta_9 - omega_Y(e_7)c_7 if row triangularization is used.

Tasks:

1. Define a predetermined core for each selected block:

     E_I^core subset I.

   E_I^core may depend on:

     H,c_7,alpha_9,beta_9^tri,N_sm,chart data.

   It must not depend on:

     Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2},W_2,extracted atoms,live residuals.

2. State D4CoeffCoreGood on E_I^core:

     |H| >= Q^{-A},

     |c_7|, |c_7^{-1}|,
     |alpha_9|, |alpha_9^{-1}|,
     |beta_9^tri| <= Q^A,

     N_sm >= Q^{-A}.

3. Prove/reduce D4CoeffCoreRouting_2Y:

   For selected routed blocks:

     |E_I^core| >= R^{-C}|I|.

   If this fails, route:
     H-degenerate branch;
     coefficient-degenerate branch;
     N_sm cutoff design failure;
     alternate chart/renormalization.

4. Use FiniteAtlasRiesz_2Y vector Remez:

   For every E subset I with |E|>=R^{-C}|I|:

     int_E W_2 >= Q^{-C} int_I W_2.

5. Prove CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer:

   From E_I^core and vector Remez:

     int_I N_sm |H|^2 W_2
       >=
     Q^{-A} int_{E_I^core} W_2
       >=
     Q^{-A'} int_I W_2.

6. Scalar component selection.

   After weighted W_2 mass, choose fixed:

     G_* in {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}

   at smooth aggregate stage or pre-extraction block class.

   Do not choose atomwise.

7. Scalar O2/O3 input:

     W_* = N_sm |H|^2 |G_*|^2.

   Bandwidth:

     d_0^(2)=d_N+2d_H+2 max(d_GammaG,d_GammaG2).

   Verify d_0^(2)<=d_max or give corrected fixed d_max.

8. Failure routing:

   - E_I^core too small:
       transfer invalid; reroute chart/core;

   - H small on most blocks:
       HNonAbsorption or alternate D4 chart;

   - N_sm removes core:
       smooth extraction design failure;

   - vector Remez fails:
       finite-atlas degeneracy;

   - scalar component chosen atomwise:
       invalid moving selector.

Deliverable:
  D4CoeffCoreRouting_2Y + CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y theorem package.
```

## Assistant (2026-04-28 15:22:00)

_Thought for 12s_

Yes — but only if we make the bridge through the **rank selector** and row-normalize first.

The clean connection is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{new “rank two”}
=
\operatorname{rank}
\left(
\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}
\right)=2
}
\]

with the row-normalized D4 matrix

\[
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7.
\]

So the bridge to the existing paper is:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\operatorname{rank}(M_{\D4}^{\tri})=2
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
}
\]

This is exactly the “rank two” that should connect to the paper’s existing rank-selector language, provided the paper’s rank-two branch is formulated as **two surviving D4 coordinates after quotienting by actual/local source freedom**. The latest agent results explicitly frame this through bounded dual rows, triangular row normalization, and the corrected D4 matrix. 

# The clean bridge theorem

The paper should probably use something like:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RankTwoBridge}_{Y12}.
}
\]

Statement:

Assume the row-normalized live fiber

\[
(e_7,e_Y)
\]

has bounded dual rows

\[
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y
\]

with

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and assume the five row blockers vanish, so

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then the projected D4 map has rank two:

\[
\operatorname{rank}
\left(
\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}^{\tri}
\right)=2.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

Then D4 supplies the honest plain vector wall:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

under coefficient routing.

# Why this is cleaner than the old rank-two language

The earlier “rank two” language in the paper may have been vulnerable to three ambiguities:

1. **Raw versus quotient rank.**  
   The relevant rank is not the raw rank of two formal D4 carriers. It is the rank after quotienting by actual/local source freedom:

   \[
   \Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}.
   \]

2. **Raw \(Y12\) row versus row-normalized \(Y12\) row.**  
   The correct second coordinate is not necessarily the raw \(\omega_Y\)-row. It is

   \[
   \ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
   \]

   So all rank-two and rank-one formulas should use

   \[
   \beta_9^{\tri}.
   \]

3. **Plain wall versus jet wall.**  
   Rank two gives

   \[
   H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
   \]

   not

   \[
   H(\Gamma_G,D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
   \]

   The derivative terms enter only through LocalMarkov / finite-atlas Riesz, not through D4.

# How it connects to existing branches

The bridge should be:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
\Rightarrow
\text{existing rank-two branch}.
}
\]

If instead:

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then the paper’s **rank-one fallback** should activate, with the corrected triangularized ratio:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

If:

\[
(0,1)\in B_Y
\quad\text{or}\quad
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2,
\]

then the current \(Y12\) branch dies locally.

So the clean bridge table is:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
B_Y & \text{paper branch}\\
\hline
0 & \text{rank-two D4 vector wall}\\
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y) & \text{rank-one moving-wall fallback}\\
\mathbb C(0,1) & \text{vertical }Y12\text{ death}\\
\mathbb C^2 & \text{live fiber absorbed}
\end{array}
\]

# Important caveat

If the paper already has a “rank-two norm route” that was marked as a no-go, do **not** identify this new branch with that no-go route unless the objects match.

This new rank two is not “rank two norm control” in the abstract. It is specifically:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank two of the row-normalized D4 map after quotienting by }B_Y.
}
\]

That is much cleaner and more local. It may supersede or bypass the older rank-two norm route, but it should not be conflated with it.

# Bottom line

Yes. The bridge is clean if the paper defines rank two as:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}
\left(
\Pi_{\mathbb C^2/B_Y}M_{\D4}^{\tri}
\right)=2.
}
\]

Then the new work supplies the missing local criterion:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RowExactness}_{2Y}\Rightarrow B_Y=0.
}
\]

That makes the existing rank-two branch activate honestly, with the corrected plain wall:

\[
\boxed{
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}
\]

The agents have reduced the local side to five row blockers plus live-row survival, and they have clarified the triangularized coefficient bookkeeping \(\beta_9^{\tri}\).

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \mathrm{RowExactness}_{2Y}
}

Specifically:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(D_{\actual}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[

\[

are all polynomially good.

\[
\Gamma_G\text{ in }H^2,
\qquad

}
