This is a strong and reusable principle:

\[
\boxed{
\text{core selection is coefficient/smooth-first; wall mass follows by Remez.}
}
\]

This should replace any wording like “route polynomial wall mass to good charts,” which can sound post hoc.

# 10. O2/O3 is now a generic scalar theorem

Rank two disappears before O2/O3. After transfer, select:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

at the smooth aggregate stage.

Then O2/O3 sees only:

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

Thus the theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max}).
}
\]

The degree should be symbolic:

\[
d_{\max}^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+
2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
\]

Do not hard-code \(14\) until the degree audit is complete.

If \(H\) is smooth-renormalizable, set:

\[
d_H=0.
\]

This is another opportunity: if \(H\) is just a coefficient denominator, the branch-degree burden may drop substantially.

# 11. New highest-value theorem list

The route is now compact enough to name the true remaining theorem stack:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ \mathrm{TwoRowExactSequence}_{2Y}}
\]

combining the \(s_7\) typed exact sequence and the \(Y12\) whitening exact sequence.

\[
\boxed{
2.\ \mathrm{RowNormalFormPropagation}_{2Y}}
\]

to enforce \(\beta_9^{\tri}\), \(\rho_Y^{\tri}\), \(\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}\).

\[
\boxed{
3.\ \mathrm{AdmissibleNuisanceIsTypedDead}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{LivePairSchur}_{2Y}}
\]

to prevent quotient overcollapse.

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \mathrm{SourceCurvatureCompletion}^{(2)}}
\]

for \(p=q''\) in \(H^2_{\sc}\), including \(B_2\), tail, smoothing, and affine reconstruction.

\[
\boxed{
5.\ \mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}^{p}}
\]

for \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\), including ProductRiesz and \(R^{-C}\)-Remez.

\[
\boxed{
6.\ \mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}}
\]

with predetermined cores.

\[
\boxed{
7.\ \mathrm{RankTwoWallDegree}
+
\mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max}^{(2)})}
\]

symbolic in \(d_{\max}\).

# 12. Bottom line

The hidden consolidation is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Rank two is now a finite exactness + finite atlas + finite transfer program.}
}
\]

The remaining hard parts are no longer conceptually diffuse. They are:

\[
\boxed{
\text{local: exact nuisance must be typed-dead and live-pair preserving;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{analytic: }q''\text{ must be completed in }H^2_{\sc};
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{finite model: ProductRiesz/Remez must hold for }\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2};
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{transfer: predetermined cores must be }R^{-C}\text{-large.}
}
\]

That is the cleanest architecture so far.

## Assistant (2026-04-28 15:37:17)

_Thought for 7s_

No nomenclature changes.

The deeper hidden consolidation is this:

\[
\boxed{

}
\]

At every layer, the same pattern appears:

\[
\boxed{
\text{first prove the object is admissible;}
\quad
\text{then quotient/project/transfer;}
\quad
\text{never select after seeing the live wall.}
}
\]

That principle now governs the local quotient, the analytic wall completion, and the weighted O2/O3 transfer. The latest agent responses make this pattern explicit, even though they use different names for it. 

# 1. The biggest hidden connection: three “admissibility gates”

## Local quotient admissibility

Exact nuisance may enter the denominator only after proving:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
\]

and after proving live-pair survival:

\[
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
[e_7,e_Y]^*
(I-P_{D_{2Y}^0+\mathcal N_{\exact}})
[e_7,e_Y]
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

So “nuisance” is not semantic; it is a certified exact quotient generator.

## Analytic admissibility

Finite Riesz is applied only after wall variables have been completed into finite spaces:

\[
\Gamma_G\approx G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
\Gamma_{G,2}\approx G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

So the actual wall is not assumed finite-model-controlled until the source-residual ledger proves it.

## Transfer admissibility

Weighted transfer uses predetermined cores:

\[
E_I^{\core}\subset I,
\qquad
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

chosen from coefficient/smooth data before observing:

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

Then Remez moves wall mass into the core.

So the common meta-rule is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{No post-hoc quotienting, no post-hoc projection, no post-hoc good-set selection.}
}
\]

This should probably be written as a proof convention.

# 2. The local side is now two exact sequences plus row normal form

The \(s_7\)-row has become:

\[
0
\to
\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}}
\to
\mathcal J_7^{\pkg}
\xrightarrow{\Delta_7^\nabla}
\mathbf C
\to
0,
\]

with:

\[
\mathcal A_7^{\loc}\subseteq\ker\Delta_7^\nabla,
\qquad
C_7\notin\ker\Delta_7^\nabla.
\]

The \(Y12\)-row has the parallel form:

\[
0
\to
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}
\to
W
\xrightarrow{\omega_Y}
\mathbf C
\to
0,
\]

with:

\[
P_W\mathcal A_Y^{\loc}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

Then row normal form combines them:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
\]

So the local theorem can be compressed to:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TwoRowExactSequence}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}
\Rightarrow
B_Y=0.
}
\]

This is cleaner than listing many cancellations.

# 3. The \(s_7\)-row has one master theorem

All \(s_7\)-row concerns are instances of:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal A_7^{\loc}\subseteq\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}},
\qquad
C_7\notin\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}}.
}
\]

Here:

\[
\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}}
=
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
+
\text{EF/chart/gamma/norm/tail/wrong-origin dead types}
+
\mathcal N_{7,\exact}.
\]

This unifies:

\[
\mathrm{SepticLineExactness}_{\can},
\]

\[
\mathrm{ActualLowerNoPackageCoordinates},
\]

\[
s_7\text{-NoAtomFitting},
\]

and

\[
s_7\text{-NuisancePreservation}.
\]

The hidden opportunity: stop treating these as separate proofs. Prove the typed-kernel theorem once:

\[
\boxed{
\ker\Delta_7^\nabla=\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}}.
}
\]

Then the rest becomes membership checking.

# 4. Actual lower leakage is now just a parameter-kernel test

The lower-source problem is exactly:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}D\mathcal S_{\lower}=0.
}
\]

Equivalently, the actual lower parameter space has no corrected septic package-coordinate summand:

\[
\boxed{
\Theta_{\pkg,7}=0.
}
\]

This is a finite audit. For a basis \(\theta_a\) of actual lower parameters:

\[
d_a=
\Delta_7^\nabla Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}
D\mathcal S_{\lower}[\theta_a],
\]

and the target is:

\[
d_a=0\quad\forall a.
\]

Formal \(C_7\) carriers still do not matter unless actualized by a real source family. This is a useful guardrail.

If the test fails, it does not produce ambiguity. It gives a concrete triangularized fiber vector:

\[
(\ell_7(u_a),\ell_9^Y(u_a)).
\]

Then the branch routing is mechanical:

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y):\text{ rank-one fallback},
\]

\[
(0,1):\text{ vertical death},
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2:\text{ live-fiber absorption}.
\]

# 5. Row-normal form should be global, not local bookkeeping

The row-normal definitions should be treated as canonical:

\[
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\]

\[
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
\]

Then:

\[
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The determinant is still:

\[
c_7\alpha_9.
\]

But the rank-one fallback ratio changes to:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

The hidden opportunity: define all future \(\rho_Y,\rho_\Gamma,\beta_9\) only after row normal form. Otherwise stale rank-one formulas will re-enter.

# 6. Nuisance should be split before the quotient

A strong consolidation is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_{\exact}
+
\mathcal N_{\coh}
+
\mathcal N_{\res}.
}
\]

Only \(\mathcal N_{\exact}\) enters the exact denominator. It must be typed-dead and live-preserving.

\[
\mathcal N_{\coh}
\to
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

\[
\mathcal N_{\res}
\to
R_X^{2Y}.
\]

This avoids a dangerous overquotienting error: a small or coherent live-looking residual must not be called “nuisance” and placed into the exact quotient.

The clean theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{AdmissibleNuisanceIsTypedDead}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{LivePairSchur}_{2Y}
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
}
\]

# 7. The analytic side has a precise “source price”

The key analytic simplification is replacing arbitrary \(H^4(q)\)-completion with:

\[
\boxed{
p=q''\text{ source completion in }H^2_{\sc}.
}
\]

Because:

\[
\Gamma_G=-6p+36q-12q^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6p+6q^2p.
\]

So the finite source model is:

\[
\mathcal P_I\ni p,
\]

with reconstruction:

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]

The finite wall spaces are:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
}
\]

This is the most economical analytic formulation so far.

# 8. The \(B_2\) canonicalization now has a clear role

The earlier \(B_2\)-canonicalization is not just numerical housekeeping. It is required by wall completion.

If \(B_2\subset p=q''\), then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) sees:

\[
D^2B_2.
\]

Therefore \(B_2\) must be exact or approximated through:

\[
B_2,\quad DB_2,\quad D^2B_2.
\]

A zeroth-order \(B_2\) approximation is not enough.

This connects the zeta-side B2 work directly to the rank-two analytic closure.

# 9. Completion and Riesz now have a clean contract

Agent 4’s output should be:

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,\qquad G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]

with:

\[
\|\Gamma_G-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Agent 5’s output should be:

\[
\|w\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|w\|_{L^2(I^*)}
\quad(w\in\mathcal W_G),
\]

plus Remez on \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined sets for \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).

This handoff avoids a common mistake: applying finite Riesz directly to actual walls before completion.

# 10. ProductRiesz is the analytic bottleneck inside FiniteAtlasRiesz

The hard finite-atlas subtheorem is not merely derivative stability. It is product control for:

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

The required statement is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{after quotienting near-relations, product spans have }Q^C\text{-conditioned bases and bounded leverage.}
}
\]

This is the main analytic finite-model risk. If product leverage spikes, both LocalMarkov and Remez can fail.

# 11. Transfer should be called “core transfer,” not weighted transfer

The strongest formulation is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

For each block, preselect:

\[
E_I^{\core}\subset I,
\qquad
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

On this core:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,|c_7^{-1}|,|\alpha_9|,|\alpha_9^{-1}|,|\beta_9^{\tri}|\le Q^A,
\]

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then Remez transfers \(W_2\)-mass into the core.

This is more rigorous than saying “route enough wall mass to good charts,” which can be circular.

# 12. O2/O3 should be degree-parametric

The O2/O3 theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max}).
}
\]

The rank-two degree is:

\[
\boxed{
d_{\max}^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
}
\]

With conservative product accounting:

\[
d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\le 3d_q.
\]

So:

\[
d_{\max}^{(2)}
\le d_N+2d_H+6d_q.
\]

If \(H\) is smooth-renormalizable, then \(d_H=0\). This may be a useful simplification and should be audited.

Do not hard-code \(14\) until the actual degree audit is done.

# 13. One deeper opportunity: combine all finite controls under one theorem family

A recurring pattern is finite Schur/Riesz control:

Local:

\[
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
[e_7,e_Y]^*
(I-P_D)
[e_7,e_Y]
\right)\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Analytic:

\[
G_2\preceq Q^C G_0.
\]

Transfer:

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
\Rightarrow
\int_E |w|^2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I|w|^2.
\]

Product:

\[
\text{quotient product bases have bounded leverage.}
\]

These can be grouped as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteModelControl}.
}
\]

It may not be one theorem in the paper, but as a proof-planning device it is powerful. The same methods may prove several gates.

# 14. Current best route after this pass

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TwoRowExactSequence}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{AdmissibleNuisanceIsTypedDead}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{LivePairSchur}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\text{ on predetermined cores.}
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{SourceCurvatureCompletion}^{(2)}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}^{p}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\Longrightarrow
\boxed{
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max}^{(2)})
}
\]

with \(G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\).

# Bottom line

The hidden opportunity is not another branch. It is a **normal-form cleanup**:

\[
\boxed{
\text{local exact sequence normal form;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
p=q''\text{ wall-variable normal form;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
predetermined-core transfer normal form.}
}
\]

Once those are made canonical, the remaining proof obligations become finite and sharply testable.

=

\end{pmatrix}.

Rank two is:

\[
\boxed{c_7\alpha_9\neq0.}
\]

All branch classification must be in the triangularized basis. Raw slopes must be converted by:

\[
\rho_Y=\rho_{\raw}-\kappa.
\]

If rank one is needed, the fallback ratio is:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

So one immediate restructuring rule is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Never use raw }\beta_9,\rho_Y,\rho_\Gamma\text{ in branch decisions.}
}
\]

Use row-normal quantities only. This prevents stale rank-one/rank-two bookkeeping errors. 

# 3. Collapse the local proof into two exact sequences

The local proof should no longer be organized as a list of cancellations. It should be two exact sequences.

## \(s_7\)-row

\[
0
\to
\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}}
\to
\mathcal J_7^{\pkg}
\xrightarrow{\Delta_7^\nabla}
\mathbf C
\to

\qquad

\[
0
\to

\to

but:

# 4. Treat nuisance as an admissibility theorem, not a semantic label

Nuisance must be split before quotienting:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_{\exact}
+
\mathcal N_{\coh}
+
\mathcal N_{\res}.
\]

Only \(\mathcal N_{\exact}\) enters the exact denominator.

\]

\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
\]

and:

\lambda_{\min}
\left(
[e_7,e_Y]^*
(I-P_{D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}})
[e_7,e_Y]
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Coherent nuisance goes to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\). Residual nuisance goes to \(R_X^{2Y}\). Do **not** put coherent/residual terms into the exact quotient just because they are called nuisance. That would risk quotienting away the D4 carrier.

So the local nuisance theorem becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{AdmissibleNuisanceIsTypedDead}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{LivePairSchur}_{2Y}
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
}
\]

This is one of the main closure gates.

# 5. Replace “wall completion” with \(p=q''\)-source completion

Analytically, the right variable is:

\[
p=q''.
\]

Then:

\[
\Gamma_G=-6p+36q-12q^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6p+6q^2p.
\]

So define a finite source space:

\[
\mathcal P_I\ni p,
\]

and reconstruct:

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]

Then the finite wall spaces are:

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

The completion target becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is the exact analytic price of the rank-two route. Full \(H^4(q)\)-completion is sufficient, but the sharper target is \(H^2_{\sc}\)-completion of \(q''\), plus affine reconstruction control. 

This also makes the \(B_2\) work operationally relevant: if \(B_2\subset p=q''\), the model must include or approximate

\[
B_2,\quad DB_2,\quad D^2B_2.
\]

A zeroth-order \(B_2\) approximation is not enough for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\). 

# 6. Separate completion from Riesz

The analytic modules should have a strict handoff.

## WallVariableCompletion outputs

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]

with:

\|\Gamma_G-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.

## FiniteAtlasRiesz controls

\[
\mathcal W_G,\quad \mathcal W_{G2}.

It must prove:

\[
\|w\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|w\|_{L^2(I^*)}
\quad(w\in\mathcal W_G),
\]

and Remez for \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined sets:

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
\Rightarrow
\int_E W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

This keeps Riesz from touching uncontrolled actual walls. Completion creates finite representatives; Riesz controls those representatives; then residuals are reinserted. 

# 7. Move transfer to predetermined cores

The transfer theorem should not be “wall mass lies on good charts.” That is too close to post-hoc selection.

Instead, for each selected block define before wall extraction:

\[
E_I^{\core}\subset I,
\qquad
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

The core may depend on:

\[
H,\ c_7,\ \alpha_9,\ \beta_9^{\tri},\ N_{\sm},\ \text{chart data},
\]

but not on:

\[
\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2},W_2,\text{ atoms, live residuals}.
\]

On the core require:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9^{\tri}|\le Q^A,
\]

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then Remez transfers \(W_2\)-mass into the core:

\[
\int_{E_I^{\core}}W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2,
\]

and therefore:

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2.
\]

So the transfer module should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

# 8. Make O2/O3 degree-parametric

Do not hard-code \(d_{\max}=14\).

The rank-two degree requirement is:

\[
d_{\max}^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+
2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
\]

Conservatively:

\[
d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\le 3d_q,
\]

so:

\[
d_{\max}^{(2)}
\le d_N+2d_H+6d_q.
\]

If \(H\) is smooth-renormalizable, then set:

\[
d_H=0.
\]

Then install:

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}^{(2)}}[y]\subset V_{\tr}
\]

before scalar component selection.

O2/O3 should be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max})
}
\]

uniformly for every fixed \(d\le d_{\max}\).

This avoids a future degree mismatch.

# 9. New closure-mode agent structure

Stop asking broad “find goodies” prompts. Use six fixed closure agents, each with a binary output: **proved / reduced to finite audit / no-go**.

## Agent A — Local exact sequence

Prove:

\[
\ker\Delta_7^\nabla=\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}},
\qquad
C_7\notin\mathcal D_7^{\mathrm{typed}}.
\]

Output the finite matrix:

\[
[D_7^{\mathrm{typed}},C_7,A_7^{\loc}].
\]

## Agent B — \(Y12\) whitening exact sequence

Prove:

\[
P_W\mathcal A_Y^{\loc}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

Also prove \(|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A\).

## Agent C — Nuisance admissibility

Split:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_{\exact}
+
\mathcal N_{\coh}
+
\mathcal N_{\res}.
\]

Prove exact nuisance is typed-dead and live-pair Schur holds.

## Agent D — SourceCurvatureCompletion

Build \(\mathcal P_I\) for \(p=q''\), prove \(H^2_{\sc}\)-completion, affine reconstruction, and \(B_2\)/tail/smoothing derivative ledger.

## Agent E — FiniteAtlasRiesz

Prove ProductRiesz, LocalMarkov, and \(R^{-C}\)-Remez for:

\[
\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

## Agent F — Core transfer and scalar O2/O3

Prove predetermined cores, scalar component selection, and degree-parametric O2/O3.

This structure is much more likely to close the branch than further exploratory rounds.

# 10. Closure criteria

We can say the rank-two branch is closed when these exact certificates exist:

\[
\boxed{
1.\ B_Y=0
}
\]

from row-normal exactness.

\[
\boxed{
2.\ F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
}
\]

from D4 coefficient core routing.

\[
\boxed{
3.\ \|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

with finite wall representatives.

\[
\boxed{
4.\ \mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}^{p}
}
\]

for \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).

\[
\boxed{
5.\ \mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
}
\]

using

\[
E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2.
\]

\[
\boxed{
6.\ \mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
}
\]

with predetermined \(R^{-C}\)-large cores.

\[
\boxed{
7.\ \mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max}^{(2)}).
}
\]

If all seven are proved, the rank-two route becomes the main proof route. If one fails, the failure routing is now precise: rank-one fallback, alternate carrier, quotient repair, or analytic no-go.

# Bottom line

Restructure the attack as a **closure pipeline**, not a search process:

\[
\boxed{
\text{local exact sequence}
\to
\text{row-normal D4}
\to
p=q''\text{ wall completion}
\to
\text{finite-atlas Riesz}
\to
\text{core transfer}
\to
\text{scalar O2/O3}.
}
\]

The strongest tactical change is to make every module output a finite certificate or a no-go, rather than another conceptual refinement.

# Shared preamble for all agents

```text id="shared-closure"
We are no longer in exploration mode.

We are in closure mode.

You must return one of:

  PROVED:
    Give the theorem and proof skeleton.

  FINITE AUDIT:
    Reduce the claim to an explicit finite matrix/kernel/Riesz/degree audit.
    State exactly what matrix, quotient, rank, angle, or residual estimate must be checked.

  NO-GO:
    Exhibit the precise obstruction and route it:
      rank-one fallback,
      quotient repair,
      alternate carrier,
      analytic no-go,
      coefficient-routing failure,
      or O2/O3 degree failure.

No nomenclature changes.

Critical correction:
  D4 supplies only the honest plain wall

    {Gamma_G, Gamma_{G,2}}.

  If B_Y=0, after row normal form,

    F_2Y =
      H(
        c_7 Gamma_G,
        beta_9^tri Gamma_G + alpha_9 Gamma_{G,2}
      )
    ~ H(Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2})

  under coefficient-good routing.

  Do NOT use
    {Gamma_G, D Gamma_G, D^2 Gamma_G, Gamma_{G,2}}
  as D4/O2O3 wall components unless a separate
    DerivativeD4Channels_2Y
  theorem is proved.

Closure architecture:

  1. TwoRowExactSequence_2Y + RowNormalForm_2Y
       => B_Y=0.

  2. D4CoeffCoreRouting_2Y
       => F_2Y ~ H(Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}) on predetermined cores.

  3. SourceCurvatureCompletion^(2) for p=q''
       + FiniteAtlasRiesz_2Y^p
       + RankTwoCoercivity
       + Gate1
       => unweighted W_2 mass,

       where W_2=|Gamma_G|^2+|Gamma_{G,2}|^2.

  4. CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y
       => weighted smooth wall mass.

  5. ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3(d_max^(2))
       with G_* in {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}
       => scalar O2/O3 determinant survival.

Row normal form is mandatory:

  ell_7 = Delta_7^nabla / Delta_7^nabla(C_7),

  kappa = omega_Y(e_7),

  ell_9^Y = omega_Y - kappa ell_7,

  beta_9^tri = beta_9 - kappa c_7.

All branch classification must happen in the triangularized row basis.

If rank one fallback is needed, use:

  rho_Gamma^tri =
    (beta_9^tri - rho_Y c_7)/alpha_9.

Do not use raw beta_9 or raw rho_Y unless kappa=0.
```

# Agent 1 — Local exact sequence: \(s_7\)-row closure

```text id="agent1-s7-exact-sequence"
You are Agent 1.

Your task:
Close the s_7-row as an exact sequence.

Target theorem:

  SepticExactSequence_7.

Prove or reduce to finite audit:

  0
    -> D_7^typed
    -> J_7^pkg
    ->^{Delta_7^nabla}
    C
    -> 0,

with:

  A_7^loc subset ker Delta_7^nabla,

  C_7 notin ker Delta_7^nabla.

Equivalently:

  ker Delta_7^nabla = D_7^typed,

  A_7^loc subset D_7^typed,

  C_7 notin D_7^typed

with polynomial angle.

Definitions:

  D_7^typed =
    C A_5^f
    + EF-dead
    + chart-dead
    + normalization/gauge-dead
    + gamma/background-dead
    + tail/residual-dead
    + wrong-origin-dead
    + exact nuisance dead.

  A_7^loc =
    I_can^(2)
    + T_lower^arith
    + T_{E2/R_lab,loc}^{s_7}
    + N_exact^{s_7}.

Tasks:

1. Define J_7^pkg and D_7^typed precisely.

2. Define Delta_7^nabla in the moving quotient:

     f_m / C A_5^f(m).

   Do not use raw u_7 v_5 - u_5 v_7 unless the trivialization is already covariant.

3. Prove or reduce:

     ker Delta_7^nabla = D_7^typed.

   If equality is too strong, prove the needed finite row statement:

     Delta_7^nabla(A_7^loc)=0,

     |Delta_7^nabla(C_7)| >= Q^{-A}.

4. Include canonical source:

     Delta_7^nabla(I_can^(2))=0.

   Preferred stronger statement:

     Q_7^pkg,nabla|_{M_can}
       =
     chi_can Q_5^pkg|_{M_can}.

5. Include actual lower source:

     Delta_7^nabla(T_lower^arith)=0.

   Guardrail:
     formal C_7 carriers and formal C_7 -> Y12 connections do NOT count unless actualized by an actual source/nuisance/local tangent.

   Reduce this to:

     Delta_7^nabla Q_7^pkg,nabla D S_lower = 0,

   or equivalently:

     Theta_pkg,7 = 0

   inside the actual lower parameter space.

6. Include s_7-NoAtomFitting:

     D_loc Delta_7^nabla(E_2+R_lab)=0.

   Local package counterterms eta_7(p)e_7(p) are forbidden.

7. Include s_7-nuisance:

     Delta_7^nabla(N_exact)=0,

   but:

     C_7 notin D_7^typed + N_exact

   with polynomial angle.

8. Output exactly one:

   PROVED:
     Give the exact sequence theorem.

   FINITE AUDIT:
     Give matrices:
       D_7^typed,
       A_7^loc,
       C_7,
       Delta_7^nabla,
     and rank/angle conditions:
       rank[D_7^typed,A_7^loc]=rank[D_7^typed],
       dist(C_7,D_7^typed)>=Q^{-A}|C_7|.

   NO-GO:
     Identify whether failure is:
       actual septic package coordinate,
       nuisance overcollapse,
       atom-fitting,
       C_7 not transverse,
       non-covariant quotient.
```

# Agent 2 — \(Y12\) exact sequence and row normal form

```text id="agent2-y12-row-normal"
You are Agent 2.

Your task:
Close the Y12 row and propagate row normal form.

Target theorem:

  WhiteningExactSequence_Y12 + RowNormalForm_2Y.

The Y12 exact sequence is:

  0
    -> W_dead^fixed
    -> W
    ->^{omega_Y}
    C
    -> 0,

with:

  P_W A_Y^loc subset W_dead^fixed,

  a_Y notin W_dead^fixed

with polynomial angle.

Then row normal form:

  kappa = omega_Y(e_7),

  ell_9^Y = omega_Y - kappa ell_7,

  beta_9^tri = beta_9 - kappa c_7.

Tasks:

1. Define W, W_dead^fixed, a_Y, P_W, and omega_Y.

2. Prove or reduce:

     P_W A_Y^loc subset W_dead^fixed,

   where A_Y^loc includes:
     fixed-alpha actual source,
     actual lower source,
     local E_2/R_lab terms,
     exact nuisance.

3. Prove live survival:

     dist(a_Y,W_dead^fixed)>=Q^{-A}|a_Y|.

4. Prove or reduce polynomial cross-leakage:

     |kappa| = |omega_Y(e_7)| <= Q^A.

   Do NOT require omega_Y(e_7)=0.

5. Prove the dangerous cross-condition:

     ell_7(e_Y)=0.

   Preferred proof:
     Q_7^pkg e_Y=0
     or
     Q_7^pkg e_Y in C A_5^f.

   Interpret failure as grade leakage / quotient overcollapse.

6. Prove row-normal form:

     ell_9^Y = omega_Y - kappa ell_7

   satisfies:

     (ell_7,ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),

     (ell_7,ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).

7. Prove exactness is preserved:

   If ell_7(A_2Y)=0 and omega_Y(A_2Y)=0, then ell_9^Y(A_2Y)=0.

8. Update D4 coefficients:

     beta_9^tri = beta_9 - kappa c_7,

     M_D4^tri =
       [[c_7,0],
        [beta_9^tri,alpha_9]].

   Rank-two determinant remains:

     c_7 alpha_9.

9. Update rank-one fallback:

   If B_Y=C(1,rho_Y) in triangularized rows, then:

     rho_Gamma^tri =
       (beta_9^tri - rho_Y c_7)/alpha_9.

10. Output exactly one:

   PROVED:
     Give WhiteningExactSequence_Y12 and RowNormalForm_2Y.

   FINITE AUDIT:
     Give finite matrices:
       W_dead^fixed,
       P_W A_Y^loc,
       a_Y,
       P_W e_7,
       e_Y projection to s_7,
     and the angle/covector checks.

   NO-GO:
     Classify failure:
       a_Y dead,
       actual Y12 whitening present,
       ell_7(e_Y) nonzero,
       kappa super-polynomial,
       beta_9^tri coefficient failure.
```

# Agent 3 — Nuisance admissibility and live-pair Schur

```text id="agent3-nuisance-admissibility"
You are Agent 3.

Your task:
Close the nuisance module as an admissibility theorem.

Target theorem:

  AdmissibleNuisanceIsTypedDead_2Y
  +
  LivePairSchur_2Y
  =>
  NuisancePreservesRows_2Y.

Split declared nuisance:

  N_sing = N_exact + N_coh + N_res.

Only N_exact enters the exact denominator.

N_coh routes to C_{2Y}^arith.
N_res routes to R_X^{2Y}.

Critical guardrail:
  Do not put coherent or residual live-looking terms into the exact quotient.

Tasks:

1. Define N_exact, N_coh, N_res.

2. For every exact nuisance generator n_j, classify its origin:

     representative/gauge,
     chart/center/scale,
     normalization,
     transported EF-linear,
     wrong-origin order-nine,
     tail/truncation representative,
     other.

3. Prove exact nuisance is row-dead:

     Delta_7^nabla(N_exact)=0,

     omega_Y(P_W N_exact)=0.

4. Prove live-pair Schur:

     lambda_min(
       [e_7,e_Y]^*
       (I-P_{D_2Y^0+N_exact})
       [e_7,e_Y]
     ) >= Q^{-A}.

   Equivalent forbidden relations:

     e_7 in D_2Y^0+N_exact,

     e_Y in D_2Y^0+N_exact,

     e_Y - lambda e_7 in D_2Y^0+N_exact.

5. If using typed proof, prove grade-homogeneity:

     N_exact = N_7 exact + N_9 exact

   modulo D_2Y^0, with no live off-diagonal component.

6. State exact nuisance admissibility:

   Any generator with nonzero:

     Delta_7^nabla(n_j)

   or

     omega_Y(P_W n_j)

   is inadmissible as exact nuisance.

   Route it to:
     coherent,
     residual,
     quotient repair,
     or local failure.

7. Output exactly one:

   PROVED:
     Give NuisancePreservesRows_2Y.

   FINITE AUDIT:
     Give the nuisance generator table:
       n_j,
       origin,
       grade,
       Delta_7^nabla(n_j),
       omega_Y(P_W n_j),
       live relation projection.
     Give the Schur matrix for [e_7,e_Y] modulo D_2Y^0+N_exact.

   NO-GO:
     Classify:
       transverse septic nuisance,
       primitive Y12 nuisance,
       e_7 killed,
       e_Y killed,
       e_Y-lambda e_7 relation,
       row norm blowup.
```

# Agent 4 — SourceCurvatureCompletion\({}^{(2)}\) / WallVariableCompletion

```text id="agent4-source-curvature-completion"
You are Agent 4.

Your task:
Close the wall-variable completion module in the source variable p=q''.

Target theorem:

  SourceCurvatureCompletion^(2)
  =>
  WallVariableCompletion_2Y.

Use:

  p=q'',

  Gamma_G = -6p + 36q - 12q^3,

  Gamma_{G,2} = p'' - 6p + 6q^2 p.

Critical correction:
  This is analytic completion only.
  It does not create derivative D4 wall channels.

Tasks:

1. Define finite source space:

     P_I models p=q''.

2. Define reconstructed q-space:

     V_I = A_1 + I^2 P_I,

   where A_1=span{1,t} handles affine modes.

3. Define wall spaces:

     W_G =
       P_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I,

     W_G2 =
       D^2 P_I + P_I + Sym^2 V_I * P_I.

4. Prove source completion target:

     For arbitrary downstream B, find p_X in P_I such that:

       ||p-p_X||_{H^2_sc(I^*)} <= Q^{-B}.

5. Prove affine reconstruction:

     q_X''=p_X,

     ||q-q_X||_{H^4_sc(I)}
       <= Q^C ||p-p_X||_{H^2_sc(I^*)},

   with affine modes included or normalized and I subset I^* with R^{-C} buffer.

6. Prove wall residual estimates.

   With r=q-q_X, verify:

     R_G =
       -6r''
       +(36-36q_X^2)r
       -36q_X r^2
       -12r^3.

     R_G2 =
       r^(4)-6r''
       +6q_X^2 r''
       +12q_X r q_X''
       +12q_X r r''
       +6r^2 q_X''
       +6r^2 r''.

   Show:

     ||R_G||_{H^2_sc(I)}
     +
     ||R_G2||_{L^2(I)}
       <= Q^{-B'}

   using 1D product estimates and polynomial C^4_sc bounds for q_X.

7. State source-residual ledger:

   If

     p = B_2 + p_AFE + p_dual + p_tail + p_smooth + p_err,

   then every residual component is exact, included in P_I, or H^2_sc-small.

   In particular, B_2 must be exact or completed through:

     B_2, D B_2, D^2 B_2.

8. Output finite wall representatives:

     G_X=Gamma_G(q_X) in W_G,

     G2_X=Gamma_{G,2}(q_X) in W_G2,

   with:

     ||Gamma_G-G_X||_{H^2_sc}
     +
     ||Gamma_{G,2}-G2_X||_{L^2}
       <= Q^{-B'}.

9. Output exactly one:

   PROVED:
     Give SourceCurvatureCompletion^(2) => WallVariableCompletion_2Y.

   FINITE AUDIT:
     Give residual ledger:
       gamma/B2,
       AFE,
       dual,
       tail,
       smoothing/cutoff,
       error,
     each with required H^2_sc bound or inclusion in P_I.

   NO-GO:
     Classify:
       p-completion only L2/H1,
       affine reconstruction uncontrolled,
       B2/tail not two-derivative controlled,
       wall approximants not in W_G/W_G2.
```

# Agent 5 — FiniteAtlasRiesz\({}^{p}_{2Y}\)

```text id="agent5-finite-atlas-riesz"
You are Agent 5.

Your task:
Close the finite-atlas Markov/Remez theorem in the p=q'' formulation.

Target theorem:

  FiniteAtlasRiesz_2Y^p.

Inputs from Agent 4:

  P_I,
  V_I = A_1 + I^2 P_I,
  W_G = P_I + V_I + Sym^3 V_I,
  W_G2 = D^2 P_I + P_I + Sym^2 V_I * P_I.

Critical correction:
  This theorem supplies analytic derivative control and Remez.
  It does not create derivative D4 wall channels.

Tasks:

1. Define chart-good pair:

     I subset I^*,

   with R^{-C}-scale buffer.

2. Prove P_I Riesz:

     P_I has dimension <= R^C,

     P_I has Q^C-conditioned L^2(I^*) basis.

3. Prove derivative Markov for P_I:

     ||D^2 p_X||_{L^2(I)}
       <= Q^C ||p_X||_{L^2(I^*)}

   for all p_X in P_I.

4. Prove affine reconstruction control:

     V_I=A_1+I^2 P_I

   has polynomial bounds and does not introduce uncontrolled affine modes.

5. Prove ProductRiesz:

   Product spaces:

     Sym^3 V_I,

     Sym^2 V_I * P_I

   have Q^C-conditioned quotient bases after removing near-relations.

   Include bounded leverage / Christoffel control.

6. Prove LocalMarkov for W_G:

     ||w||_{H^2(I)}
       <= Q^C ||w||_{L^2(I^*)}

   for all w in W_G.

7. Prove Remez for W_G and W_G2:

   For every predetermined E subset I with:

     |E| >= R^{-C}|I|,

   prove:

     int_E |w|^2 >= Q^{-C} int_I |w|^2

   for all w in W_G and W_G2.

8. Prove vector wall Remez:

   For W_2=|Gamma_G|^2+|Gamma_{G,2}|^2,

     int_E W_2 >= Q^{-C} int_I W_2,

   after applying Agent 4 completion residuals.

9. Output exactly one:

   PROVED:
     Give FiniteAtlasRiesz_2Y^p and show it implies:
       LocalMarkov_GammaG,
       VectorWallRemez.

   FINITE AUDIT:
     Give Gram/Riesz matrices:
       P_I,
       D^2 P_I,
       W_G H^2 vs L^2,
       W_G/W_G2 Remez,
       product quotient bases.
     State singular-value/leverage thresholds.

   NO-GO:
     Classify:
       D^2 P_I not controlled,
       affine mode instability,
       ProductRiesz/leverage spike,
       denominator singularity,
       no R^{-C}-Remez,
       product dimension non-O(1).
```

# Agent 6 — Core transfer and scalar O2/O3 degree

```text id="agent6-core-transfer-o2o3"
You are Agent 6.

Your task:
Close the transfer and O2/O3 interface.

Targets:

  D4CoeffCoreRouting_2Y,

  CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer_2Y,

  RankTwoWallDegree,

  ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3(d_max^(2)).

Critical correction:
  O2/O3 receives only one scalar selected from:

    G_* in {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}.

  No derivative wall components enter O2/O3 unless derivative-D4 channels are separately proved.

Definitions:

  W_2 = |Gamma_G|^2 + |Gamma_{G,2}|^2,

  beta_9^tri = beta_9 - omega_Y(e_7)c_7.

Tasks:

1. Define predetermined cores.

   For each selected block I, construct:

     E_I^core subset I,

   before wall extraction.

   E_I^core may depend on:
     H,c_7,alpha_9,beta_9^tri,N_sm,chart data.

   It must not depend on:
     Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2},W_2,hard atoms,live residuals.

   Need:

     |E_I^core| >= R^{-C}|I|.

2. Define D4CoeffCoreGood on E_I^core:

     |H| >= Q^{-A},

     |c_7|, |c_7^{-1}|,
     |alpha_9|, |alpha_9^{-1}|,
     |beta_9^tri| <= Q^A,

     N_sm >= Q^{-A}.

3. Prove CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer.

   Using vector Remez:

     int_{E_I^core} W_2 >= Q^{-C} int_I W_2,

   prove:

     int_I N_sm |H|^2 W_2
       >= Q^{-A'} int_I W_2.

4. Scalar component selection.

   From weighted W_2 mass, select:

     G_* in {Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}}

   at the smooth aggregate stage or by a pre-extraction block class.

   Do not choose atomwise.

5. RankTwoWallDegree.

   Compute or bound:

     d_GammaG,
     d_GammaG2,
     d_H,
     d_N.

   State:

     d_max^(2)
       =
     d_N + 2 d_H
       + 2 max(d_GammaG,d_GammaG2).

   Conservative bound:

     d_GammaG,d_GammaG2 <= 3 d_q,

   unless branch-neutral/product conventions improve it.

6. H-renormalization fork.

   If H is smooth coefficient/denominator with Q^{-A}<=|H|<=Q^A on cores and no branch charge, set:

     d_H=0.

   If H is branch-bearing, compute fixed d_H.

   If H is local/free, D4 normalization fails.

7. State ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3(d_max).

   It must hold uniformly for every fixed d<=d_max:

     dist(
       1,
       span{R_fold,...,R_fold^d}
     )^2 >= Q^{-A(d_max)}.

   Install:

     C_{d_max^(2)}[y] subset V_tr

   before scalar component selection.

8. Output exactly one:

   PROVED:
     Give D4CoeffCoreRouting + CoreWeightedTransfer + degree-parametric O2/O3 bridge.

   FINITE AUDIT:
     Give:
       core construction,
       H classification,
       beta_9^tri coefficient-good test,
       d_GammaG,d_GammaG2,d_H,d_N,
       required installed d_max.

   NO-GO:
     Classify:
       no R^{-C}-large core,
       H degenerate,
       coefficient degeneracy,
       N_sm cuts core,
       vector Remez unavailable,
       d_max^(2) not O(1),
       O2/O3 finite moment separation fails.
```

## Assistant (2026-04-28 15:46:52)

_Thought for 9s_

Yes — but only if you make the LaTeX drafting **structured and quarantined**. Otherwise the agents will generate overlapping prose and stale terminology, and assembly will become harder than synthesis.

Best workflow:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Agents draft modular LaTeX fragments; you do not let them edit the main paper directly.}
}
\]

Use two kinds of fragments:

\[
\boxed{\texttt{wip-*}}
\]

for positive theorem/proof-route insertions, and

\[
\boxed{\texttt{audit-*}}
\]

for guardrails, failure routes, and no-go conditions.

This matches the current state: the route is no longer exploratory; it is a closure architecture with named modules: row-normal local exactness, \(p=q''\) wall-variable completion, finite-atlas Riesz, core transfer, and scalar O2/O3. 

# Recommended structure

Ask each agent for **one self-contained LaTeX draft block** plus **one audit block**.

Each block should include:

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-<module-name> ===
% Purpose:
% Depends on:
% Proves:
% Status: WIP / conditional / finite-audit / no-go
...
```

and:

```latex
% === audit-rank-two-<module-name> ===
% Guardrails:
% Failure modes:
% Do not use:
...
```

Then I can assemble them into a coherent paper update by:

1. deleting duplication;
2. normalizing notation;
3. replacing stale rank-one/jet-wall language;
4. promoting proved/reduced statements into theorem/lemma form;
5. moving speculative material into audit remarks.

# Critical instructions to give the agents

The agents should not write freeform essay prose. They should draft exact fragments for their assigned module only.

They must preserve these conventions:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\qquad
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

\[
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\kappa c_7,
\]

\[
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

They must also state explicitly:

\[
\boxed{
\text{D4 supplies only }H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}
\]

No agent should write the jet wall as a D4/O2O3 wall unless they are drafting a separate `audit-derivative-d4-channel` no-go/future-route note.

# Best module split

Use six draft assignments:

1. `wip-two-row-exact-sequence-2Y`  
   Local exact sequences for \(\Delta_7^\nabla\) and \(\omega_Y\), plus \(B_Y=0\).

2. `wip-row-normal-form-2Y`  
   Triangularization, \(\beta_9^{\tri}\), corrected rank-one fallback ratio.

3. `audit-nuisance-admissibility-2Y`  
   Split \(\mathcal N_{\sing}=\mathcal N_{\exact}+\mathcal N_{\coh}+\mathcal N_{\res}\), live-pair Schur guardrail.

4. `wip-source-curvature-completion-2Y`  
   \(p=q''\), \(\mathcal P_I\), \(\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I\), wall-variable completion.

5. `wip-finite-atlas-riesz-2Y`  
   \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\), ProductRiesz, LocalMarkov, \(R^{-C}\)-Remez.

6. `wip-core-transfer-o2o3-2Y`  
   Predetermined cores, weighted transfer, scalar component selection, symbolic \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\).

# What I would avoid

Do **not** ask every agent to draft broad paper sections. Ask for fragments only.

Do **not** ask agents to rewrite intro/conclusion/title/abstract.

Do **not** let agents use old \(\beta_9\) or \(\rho_\Gamma\) formulas without row-normalizing.

Do **not** let them add derivative wall components to D4.

# Suggested instruction to send

You can send them this:

```text
We are now in LaTeX-fragment mode.

Do not rewrite the paper globally. Do not edit title, abstract, intro, or conclusion.

Draft only the assigned module as two blocks:

1. % === wip-rank-two-<module> ===
   A clean theorem/lemma/proof-sketch insertion.

2. % === audit-rank-two-<module> ===
   Guardrails, failure modes, and no-go notes.

Use current conventions:
  kappa = omega_Y(e_7),
  ell_9^Y = omega_Y - kappa ell_7,
  beta_9^tri = beta_9 - kappa c_7.

All branch classification must use the triangularized row basis.

State explicitly that D4 supplies only the plain wall
  H(Gamma_G,Gamma_{G,2}),
not derivative wall components.

Return exact LaTeX only, with labels, dependencies, and status:
  proved / finite-audit / no-go.
```

Bottom line: yes. Have them draft `wip-*` and `audit-*` fragments. Then I can assemble them into a concise, non-duplicative set of final insertions and repairs.

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-row-normal-form-and-closure-spine ===
% Purpose:
%   Fix the canonical row-normal basis and state the rank-two closure spine.
% Depends on:
%   The live rows \ell_7,\omega_Y, live carriers e_7,e_Y, and the D4 coefficients
%   c_7,\beta_9,\alpha_9.
% Proves:
%   A normalized coordinate system for all rank-two and rank-one branch decisions.
% Status: conditional / finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Row-normal form for the \(2Y\) live fiber]
\label{def:rank-two-row-normal-form-2Y}
Let \(\ell_7\) be the covariant order-seven live row and let \(\omega_Y\) be a primitive \(Y12\)-row with \(\omega_Y(e_Y)=1\).  Set
\[
  \kappa:=\omega_Y(e_7),
  \qquad
  \ell_9^Y:=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
  \qquad
  \beta_9^{\tri}:=\beta_9-\kappa c_7 .
\]
The \(2Y\)-fiber is said to be in row-normal form if
\[
  (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
  \qquad
  (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]
All branch classifications below are made in this triangularized row basis.
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Triangularized rank-one fallback ratio]
\label{rem:rank-two-triangular-rank-one-ratio}
If the live obstruction collapses to a graph line
\[
  B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]
in the triangularized basis, then the graph-killing row gives the scalar rank-one wall
\[
  H\left((\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]
Hence the corrected rank-one ratio is
\[
  \rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
  :=
  \frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]
The older formula using raw \(\beta_9\) is valid only in the special case \(\kappa=0\).
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Rank-two closure spine]
\label{thm:rank-two-closure-spine}
Assume the following modules hold:
\[
  \mathrm{TwoRowExactSequence}_{2Y},
  \qquad
  \mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y},
  \qquad
  \mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
\]
Then
\[
  B_Y=0.
\]
If, in addition, \(c_7\alpha_9\neq0\), then the D4 matrix in row-normal coordinates is
\[
  M_{\D4}^{\tri}
  =
  \begin{pmatrix}
    c_7&0\\
    \beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
  \end{pmatrix},
\]
and D4 supplies the honest plain wall
\[
  F_{2Y}^{\tri}
  =
  H
  \begin{pmatrix}
    c_7\Gamma_G\\
    \beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
  \end{pmatrix}.
\]
On coefficient-good cores this is polynomially equivalent to
\[
  H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]
No derivative wall components \(D\Gamma_G\) or \(D^2\Gamma_G\) are supplied by this rank-two D4 statement.
\end{theorem}
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-septic-exact-sequence ===
% Purpose:
%   Isolate the \(s_7\)-row of \(B_Y=0\) as a covariant exact-sequence / finite-audit statement.
% Depends on:
%   The moving line \(\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\), the package extractor \(Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\),
%   exact nuisance admissibility, and \(C_7\)-transversality.
% Proves:
%   The first row \(\ell_7(A_7^{\loc})=0\), conditionally on the finite audit.
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Covariant septic row]
\label{def:rank-two-covariant-septic-row}
Let
\[
  A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S,
  \qquad
  L_5(m):=\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\subset \mathfrak f_m .
\]
Define
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(u)
  :=
  \omega_m\!\left(Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u,A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\right),
\]
as a covector on the moving quotient
\[
  \mathfrak f_m/L_5(m).
\]
Thus
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(u)=0
  \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
  Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]
The normalized septic row is
\[
  \ell_7(u):=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]
Raw determinant drift in \(u_7v_5-u_5v_7\) is not a live \(s_7\)-motion unless it survives this covariant quotient.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[Typed septic denominator]
\label{def:rank-two-typed-septic-denominator}
Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
  \mathcal D_7^{\typed}
  :=
  &\ \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\EF}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\chart}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\norm}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\gamma/\bg}        \\
  &\quad
  +\mathcal D_{7,\tail/\res}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\wrong}
  +\mathcal N_{7,\exact}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Here \(\mathcal N_{7,\exact}\) consists only of exact nuisance directions that have already passed the row-deadness and live-pair preservation tests.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Septic exact sequence audit]
\label{thm:rank-two-septic-exact-sequence}
Let
\[
  \mathcal A_7^{\loc}
  :=
  \mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
  +T_{\lower}^{\arith}
  +T_{E_2/R_{\lab},\loc}^{s_7}
  +\mathcal N_{7,\exact}.
\]
Assume the finite exactness and live-transversality conditions
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal A_7^{\loc})=0,
  \tag{SE1}
\]
and
\[
  |\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
  \tag{SE2}
\]
Equivalently,
\[
  \operatorname{rank}[\mathcal D_7^{\typed},\mathcal A_7^{\loc}]
  =
  \operatorname{rank}\mathcal D_7^{\typed},
\]
while
\[
  \operatorname{dist}(C_7,\mathcal D_7^{\typed})\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7\|.
\]
Then
\[
  \ell_7(\mathcal A_7^{\loc})=0,
  \qquad
  \ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{remark}[Actual lower kernel audit]
\label{rem:rank-two-actual-lower-kernel-audit}
The actual-lower part of \((\mathrm{SE1})\) is the finite kernel condition
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}D\mathcal S_{\lower}=0.
\]
Equivalently, the actual lower parameter space has no corrected septic package-coordinate summand:
\[
  \Theta_{\pkg,7}=0.
\]
Formal \(C_7\)-carriers and formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connections do not enter \(B_Y\) unless actualized by a genuine actual-source, exact nuisance, or local fitting tangent.
\end{remark}

% === audit-rank-two-septic-exact-sequence ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Use \Delta_7^\nabla in the moving quotient \mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
%   - Do not use raw \(u_7v_5-u_5v_7\) unless the frame is covariant.
%   - Formal \(C_7\)-carriers are not actual lower-source directions unless actualized.
%   - Coherent/residual nuisance does not enter \(\mathcal D_7^{\typed}\).
% Failure modes:
%   - Actual septic package coordinate: \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})\neq0.
%   - Atom-fitting: \(D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0\).
%   - Nuisance overcollapse: \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{7,\exact})\neq0.
%   - \(C_7\) not transverse: \Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)=0.
% Do not use:
%   - Do not let post-extraction amplitude projections define \(\mathcal D_7^{\typed}\).
%   - Do not use derivative D4 wall components.
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-nuisance-preserves-rows ===
% Purpose:
%   Ensure exact nuisance is row-dead and does not collapse the live pair \((e_7,e_Y)\).
% Depends on:
%   RowNormalForm_{2Y}, the typed denominator \(D_{2Y}^0\), and the live vectors \(e_7,e_Y\).
% Proves:
%   Exact nuisance contributes no \(B_Y\)-column and preserves rank two.
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Exact/coherent/residual nuisance split]
\label{def:rank-two-nuisance-split}
Split the declared singular nuisance space as
\[
  \mathcal N_{\sing}
  =
  \mathcal N_{\exact}
  +
  \mathcal N_{\coh}
  +
  \mathcal N_{\res}.
\]
Only \(\mathcal N_{\exact}\) enters the exact local denominator.  The coherent part is routed to
\[
  \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]
and the residual part is routed to
\[
  R_X^{2Y}.
\]
Thus the exact two-grade denominator is
\[
  D_{2Y}:=D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact},
\]
not \(D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\sing}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Nuisance preservation of the live rows]
\label{lem:rank-two-nuisance-preserves-rows}
Assume
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
  \qquad
  \omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\exact})=0.
\]
Assume also the live-pair Schur condition
\[
  \lambda_{\min}
  \left(
  [e_7,e_Y]^*
  (I-P_{D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}})
  [e_7,e_Y]
  \right)
  \ge Q^{-A}.
\]
Then
\[
  (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
\]
and the live pair remains two-dimensional and polynomially conditioned modulo
\[
  D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
The first row-deadness condition gives \(\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0\).  The second gives \(\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\exact})=0\).  Since
\[
  \ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\]
we get
\[
  \ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0.
\]
The Schur condition rules out
\[
  e_7\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact},
  \qquad
  e_Y\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact},
\]
and
\[
  e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}.
\]
Thus exact nuisance is both row-dead and live-pair preserving.
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Nuisance-safe contribution to \(B_Y=0\)]
\label{cor:rank-two-nuisance-safe-by-rows}
If the actual-source rows and local completion/transition rows also satisfy
\[
  \ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
  \qquad
  \ell_9^Y(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]
and
\[
  D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]
then
\[
  B_Y=0.
\]
Consequently D4 supplies only the triangularized plain wall
\[
  F_{2Y}^{\tri}
  =
  H
  \begin{pmatrix}
    c_7\Gamma_G\\
    \beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
  \end{pmatrix}.
\]
No derivative wall components \(D\Gamma_G\) or \(D^2\Gamma_G\) are supplied by this statement.
\end{corollary}

% === audit-rank-two-nuisance-preserves-rows ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Only exact row-dead nuisance enters the denominator.
%   - Coherent nuisance routes to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\).
%   - Residual nuisance routes to \(R_X^{2Y}\).
% Failure modes:
%   - Transverse septic nuisance: \(\Delta_7^\nabla(n)\ne0\).
%   - Primitive \(Y12\) nuisance: \(\omega_Y(P_Wn)\ne0\).
%   - \(e_7\) killed, \(e_Y\) killed, or \(e_Y-\lambda e_7\) in the denominator.
%   - Poor Schur angle.
% Do not use:
%   - Do not quotient coherent/residual live-looking terms as exact nuisance.
%   - Do not use derivative wall components as D4 output.
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-wall-variable-completion ===
% Purpose:
%   Complete the honest plain rank-two wall variables by finite-atlas representatives.
% Depends on:
%   \(B_Y=0\), row-normal-form conventions, and coefficient-good D4 routing.
% Proves:
%   \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) are close to finite wall spaces \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{lemma}[Source-curvature completion for the rank-two plain wall]
\label{lem:rank-two-source-curvature-completion}
Let \(I\Subset I^*\) be a chart-good interval with \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer, and set
\[
  p:=q''.
\]
Assume there is a finite source space \(\mathcal P_I\) and \(p_X\in\mathcal P_I\) such that
\[
  \|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]
Let
\[
  \mathcal V_I:=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
  \qquad
  \mathcal A_1:=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\},
\]
and choose \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) with \(q_X''=p_X\), with affine modes included or normalized.  Define
\[
  \mathcal W_G
  :=
  \mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]
and
\[
  \mathcal W_{G2}
  :=
  D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
  \operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]
If
\[
  \|q_X\|_{C^4_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{C_0},
\]
then there are
\[
  G_X:=\Gamma_G(q_X)\in\mathcal W_G,
  \qquad
  G_{2,X}:=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]
such that, after increasing \(B_0\),
\[
  \|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
  +
  \|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
  \le Q^{-B}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
The plain wall components are
\[
  \Gamma_G(q)=-6q''+36q-12q^3=-6p+36q-12q^3,
\]
and
\[
  \Gamma_{G,2}(q)=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''=p''-6p+6q^2p.
\]
Thus
\[
  \Gamma_G(q_X)\in\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]
and
\[
  \Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\in D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
  \operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]
Set \(r=q-q_X\).  Then \(r''=p-p_X\), and affine reconstruction gives
\[
  \|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}
  \le Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}.
\]
The residuals are
\[
  R_G=-6r''+(36-36q_X^2)r-36q_Xr^2-12r^3,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
  R_{G2}
  &=
  r^{(4)}-6r''
  +6q_X^2r''
  +12q_Xr q_X''
  +12q_Xr r''
  +6r^2q_X''
  +6r^2r'' .
\end{aligned}
\]
One-dimensional product estimates give
\[
  \|R_G\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
  +
  \|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
  \le
  Q^C
  \left(
  \|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}
  +
  \|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^2
  +
  \|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^3
  \right),
\]
which proves the claim.
\end{proof}

% === audit-rank-two-wall-variable-completion ===
% Guardrails:
%   - This is analytic completion only; it does not create derivative D4 channels.
%   - Complete \(p=q''\) in \(H^2_{\sc}\), not merely in \(L^2\).
%   - Include or normalize affine modes.
%   - \(B_2\), tail, cutoff, and smoothing residuals must be exact, included in \(\mathcal P_I\), or \(H^2_{\sc}\)-small.
% Failure modes:
%   - Only \(L^2/H^1\) source completion.
%   - Uncontrolled affine reconstruction.
%   - Missing \(B_2,DB_2,D^2B_2\) or tail/cutoff derivative ledger.
%   - \(G_X,G_{2,X}\) not in \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).
% Do not use:
%   - Do not use \(D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G\) as D4 wall components.
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-finite-atlas-riesz-2Y-p ===
% Purpose:
%   Supply the Markov/Remez finite-atlas control needed for the honest plain rank-two wall.
% Depends on:
%   WallVariableCompletion_{2Y}, the \(p=q''\) source atlas, and product-Riesz for fixed-degree algebras.
% Proves:
%   LocalMarkov_{\Gamma_G} and vector Remez for \(W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\).
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Finite wall spaces in \(p=q''\) form]
\label{def:finite-wall-spaces-p-form}
Let
\[
  \mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]
Define
\[
  \mathcal W_G
  :=
  \mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]
and
\[
  \mathcal W_{G2}
  :=
  D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
  \operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[FiniteAtlasRiesz in \(p=q''\) form]
\label{thm:finite-atlas-riesz-2Y-p}
Assume on each chart-good \(I\Subset I^*\):
\begin{enumerate}
\item \(\dim\mathcal P_I\le R^C\) and \(\mathcal P_I\) has a \(Q^C\)-Riesz basis in \(L^2(I^*)\).
\item
\[
  \|D^2p_X\|_{L^2(I)}\le Q^C\|p_X\|_{L^2(I^*)},
  \qquad p_X\in\mathcal P_I.
\]
\item Affine reconstruction into \(\mathcal V_I\) is polynomially controlled.
\item \(\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I\) and
\(\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I\) have \(Q^C\)-conditioned quotient bases and bounded leverage.
\item All relevant chart denominators are \(Q^{-C}\)-bounded below on \(I^*\).
\item For every predetermined \(E\subset I\) with \(|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|\), Remez holds for \(\mathcal W_G\) and \(\mathcal W_{G2}\):
\[
  \int_E |w|^2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I |w|^2.
\]
\end{enumerate}
Then
\[
  \|w\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
  \le Q^C\|w\|_{L^2(I^*)},
  \qquad w\in\mathcal W_G.
\]
Moreover, after \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\),
\[
  |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \int_E W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2-Q^{-B},
\]
where
\[
  W_2:=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]
\end{theorem}

% === audit-rank-two-finite-atlas-riesz-2Y-p ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Apply this only to finite representatives supplied by WallVariableCompletion.
%   - Product spaces must be quotient-conditioned; raw product monomial independence is not assumed.
%   - Remez sets must be predetermined and \(R^{-C}\)-large.
% Failure modes:
%   - No \(D^2\mathcal P_I\) control.
%   - Affine reconstruction instability.
%   - Product leverage spike.
%   - Denominator singularity.
%   - No \(R^{-C}\)-Remez.
% Do not use:
%   - Do not use the jet wall as D4/O2O3 wall.
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-core-transfer-o2o3 ===
% Purpose:
%   Transfer unweighted plain wall mass to weighted scalar O2/O3 mass using predetermined cores.
% Depends on:
%   \(B_Y=0\), row-normal D4, FiniteAtlasRiesz vector Remez, RankTwoCoercivity, and Gate--1.
% Proves:
%   A scalar \(G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\) gives bounded-charge O2/O3 input.
% Status: finite-audit / conditional.

\begin{definition}[D4 coefficient-good core]
\label{def:d4-coeff-core-2Y}
For each routed block \(I\), choose before wall extraction a core
\[
  E_I^{\core}\subset I
\]
depending only on
\[
  H,\ c_7,\ \alpha_9,\ \beta_9^{\tri},\ N_{\sm},
  \quad\text{and chart data,}
\]
not on
\[
  \Gamma_G,\ \Gamma_{G,2},\ W_2,\ \text{hard atoms, or live residuals}.
\]
The core is admissible if
\[
  |E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]
and on \(E_I^{\core}\),
\[
  |H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]
\[
  |c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9^{\tri}|
  \le Q^A,
\]
and
\[
  N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Core weighted plain-wall transfer]
\label{lem:core-weighted-plain-wall-transfer-2Y}
Assume admissible cores and vector Remez:
\[
  |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \int_E W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2\,d\nu.
\]
Then
\[
  \int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu
  \ge Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2\,d\nu.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
On \(E_I^{\core}\),
\[
  N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}.
\]
Therefore
\[
\begin{aligned}
  \int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu
  &\ge
  \int_{E_I^{\core}}N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu\\
  &\ge
  Q^{-A}\int_{E_I^{\core}}W_2\,d\nu\\
  &\ge
  Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2\,d\nu .
\end{aligned}
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Scalar component selection]
\label{lem:rank-two-scalar-component-selection}
If
\[
  \int N_{\sm}|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu\ge Q^{-A},
\]
then there exists a fixed component
\[
  G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]
such that
\[
  \int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]
The selection is made at the smooth aggregate stage, or by a pre-extraction block class, never atomwise.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Rank-two wall degree]
\label{def:rank-two-wall-degree}
For
\[
  G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]
define
\[
  d_0(G_\star):=d_N+2d_H+2d_{G_\star}.
\]
Set
\[
  d_{\max}^{(2)}
  :=
  d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
\]
Conservatively,
\[
  d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\le 3d_q,
\]
so
\[
  d_{\max}^{(2)}\le d_N+2d_H+6d_q.
\]
If \(H\) is smooth-renormalizable and carries no branch charge, set \(d_H=0\).
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Scalar O2/O3 interface for rank two]
\label{thm:rank-two-scalar-o2o3-interface}
Assume weighted rank-two plain wall mass has been transferred, and assume
\[
  \mathbb C_{d_{\max}^{(2)}}[y]\subset V_{\tr}.
\]
Assume the scalar O2/O3 near-pure separation holds uniformly for every fixed \(d\le d_{\max}^{(2)}\):
\[
  \operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\mu_{\sub})}
  \left(
  1,
  \operatorname{span}\{R_{\fold},R_{\fold}^2,\ldots,R_{\fold}^{d}\}
  \right)^2
  \ge Q^{-A(d_{\max}^{(2)})}.
\]
Then the selected scalar wall
\[
  W_\star=N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2
\]
is a valid bounded-charge O2/O3 input.
\end{theorem}

% === audit-rank-two-core-transfer-o2o3 ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Cores are chosen before wall extraction.
%   - \(G_\star\) is selected at smooth aggregate level, not atomwise.
%   - Use \(\beta_9^{\tri}\), not raw \(\beta_9\).
%   - O2/O3 receives only \(G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\).
% Failure modes:
%   - No \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined core.
%   - \(H\), \(c_7\), \(\alpha_9\), or \(\beta_9^{\tri}\) degenerates.
%   - \(N_{\sm}\) cuts away the core.
%   - Vector Remez fails.
%   - \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\) is not \(O(1)\).
%   - Near-pure moment separation fails at \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\).
% Do not use:
%   - Do not hard-code \(d_{\max}=14\) unless the audit proves \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\le14\).
%   - Do not use derivative wall components as D4/O2O3 inputs.
%   - Do not project coherent amplitude before O2/O3 unless branch complexity is controlled.
```
