```latex
% === wip-rank-two-row-normal-form-and-closure-spine ===
% Purpose:
%   Fix the canonical row-normal basis and state the rank-two local closure spine.
% Depends on:
%   The live rows \ell_7,\omega_Y, the live carriers e_7,e_Y, and the D4
%   coefficients c_7,\beta_9,\alpha_9.
% Proves:
%   A normalized coordinate system for all rank-two and rank-one branch decisions.
% Status: conditional / finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Row-normal form for the \(2Y\) live fiber]
\label{def:rank-two-row-normal-form-2Y}
Let \(\ell_7\) be the covariant order-seven live row and let \(\omega_Y\) be a
primitive \(Y12\)-row with \(\omega_Y(e_Y)=1\).  Set
\[
  \kappa:=\omega_Y(e_7),
  \qquad
  \ell_9^Y:=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
  \qquad
  \beta_9^{\tri}:=\beta_9-\kappa c_7 .
\]
The \(2Y\)-fiber is in row-normal form if
\[
  (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
  \qquad
  (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]
All branch classifications below are made in this triangularized row basis.
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Triangularized rank-one fallback ratio]
\label{rem:rank-two-triangular-rank-one-ratio}
If the live obstruction collapses to a graph line
\[
  B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]
in the triangularized basis, then the graph-killing row gives the scalar
rank-one wall
\[
  H\left((\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]
Hence the corrected rank-one ratio is
\[
  \rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
  :=
  \frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]
The older formula using raw \(\beta_9\) is valid only when \(\kappa=0\).
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Rank-two closure spine]
\label{thm:rank-two-closure-spine}
Assume
\[
  \mathrm{TwoRowExactSequence}_{2Y},
  \qquad
  \mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y},
  \qquad
  \mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
\]
Then
\[
  B_Y=0.
\]
If moreover \(c_7\alpha_9\ne0\), then the D4 matrix in row-normal coordinates is
\[
  M_{\D4}^{\tri}
  =
  \begin{pmatrix}
    c_7&0\\
    \beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
  \end{pmatrix},
\]
and D4 supplies the plain wall
\[
  F_{2Y}^{\tri}
  =
  H
  \begin{pmatrix}
    c_7\Gamma_G\\
    \beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
  \end{pmatrix}.
\]
On coefficient-good cores this is polynomially equivalent to
\[
  H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]
No derivative wall components \(D\Gamma_G\) or \(D^2\Gamma_G\) are supplied by
this rank-two D4 statement.
\end{theorem}
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-septic-exact-sequence ===
% Purpose:
%   Isolate the \(s_7\)-row of \(B_Y=0\) as a covariant exact-sequence /
%   finite-audit statement.
% Depends on:
%   The moving line \(\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\), the package extractor
%   \(Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\), exact nuisance admissibility, and \(C_7\)-transversality.
% Proves:
%   The first row \(\ell_7(A_7^{\loc})=0\), conditionally on the finite audit.
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Covariant septic row]
\label{def:rank-two-covariant-septic-row}
Let
\[
  A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S,
  \qquad
  L_5(m):=\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\subset \mathfrak f_m .
\]
Define
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(u)
  :=
  \omega_m\!\left(Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u,A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)\right),
\]
as a covector on the moving quotient
\[
  \mathfrak f_m/L_5(m).
\]
Thus
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(u)=0
  \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
  Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
\]
The normalized septic row is
\[
  \ell_7(u):=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]
Raw determinant drift in \(u_7v_5-u_5v_7\) is not a live \(s_7\)-motion unless
it survives this covariant quotient.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[Typed septic denominator]
\label{def:rank-two-typed-septic-denominator}
Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
  \mathcal D_7^{\typed}
  :=
  &\ \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\EF}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\chart}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\norm}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\gamma/\bg}        \\
  &\quad
  +\mathcal D_{7,\tail/\res}
  +\mathcal D_{7,\wrong}
  +\mathcal N_{7,\exact}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Here \(\mathcal N_{7,\exact}\) consists only of exact nuisance directions that
have already passed the row-deadness and live-pair preservation tests.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Septic exact sequence audit]
\label{thm:rank-two-septic-exact-sequence}
Let
\[
  \mathcal A_7^{\loc}
  :=
  \mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
  +T_{\lower}^{\arith}
  +T_{E_2/R_{\lab},\loc}^{s_7}
  +\mathcal N_{7,\exact}.
\]
Assume
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal A_7^{\loc})=0,
  \tag{SE1}
\]
and
\[
  |\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
  \tag{SE2}
\]
Equivalently,
\[
  \operatorname{rank}[\mathcal D_7^{\typed},\mathcal A_7^{\loc}]
  =
  \operatorname{rank}\mathcal D_7^{\typed},
\]
while
\[
  \operatorname{dist}(C_7,\mathcal D_7^{\typed})\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7\|.
\]
Then
\[
  \ell_7(\mathcal A_7^{\loc})=0,
  \qquad
  \ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{remark}[Actual lower kernel audit]
\label{rem:rank-two-actual-lower-kernel-audit}
The actual-lower part of \((\mathrm{SE1})\) is the finite kernel condition
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}D\mathcal S_{\lower}=0.
\]
Equivalently, the actual lower parameter space has no corrected septic
package-coordinate summand:
\[
  \Theta_{\pkg,7}=0.
\]
Formal \(C_7\)-carriers and formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connections do not enter
\(B_Y\) unless actualized by a genuine actual-source, exact nuisance, or local
fitting tangent.
\end{remark}

% === audit-rank-two-septic-exact-sequence ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Use \Delta_7^\nabla in the moving quotient \mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m).
%   - Do not use raw \(u_7v_5-u_5v_7\) unless the frame is covariant.
%   - Formal \(C_7\)-carriers are not actual lower-source directions unless actualized.
%   - Coherent/residual nuisance does not enter \(\mathcal D_7^{\typed}\).
% Failure modes:
%   - Actual septic package coordinate: \(\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})\neq0\).
%   - Atom-fitting: \(D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(E_2+R_{\lab})\neq0\).
%   - Nuisance overcollapse: \(\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{7,\exact})\neq0\).
%   - \(C_7\) not transverse: \(\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)=0\).
% Do not use:
%   - Do not let post-extraction amplitude projections define \(\mathcal D_7^{\typed}\).
%   - Do not use derivative D4 wall components.
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-nuisance-preserves-rows ===
% Purpose:
%   Ensure exact nuisance is row-dead and does not collapse the live pair \((e_7,e_Y)\).
% Depends on:
%   RowNormalForm_{2Y}, the typed denominator \(D_{2Y}^0\), and the live vectors \(e_7,e_Y\).
% Proves:
%   Exact nuisance contributes no \(B_Y\)-column and preserves rank two.
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{definition}[Exact/coherent/residual nuisance split]
\label{def:rank-two-nuisance-split}
Split the declared singular nuisance space as
\[
  \mathcal N_{\sing}
  =
  \mathcal N_{\exact}
  +
  \mathcal N_{\coh}
  +
  \mathcal N_{\res}.
\]
Only \(\mathcal N_{\exact}\) enters the exact local denominator.  The coherent
part is routed to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\), and the residual part is routed
to \(R_X^{2Y}\).  Thus
\[
  D_{2Y}:=D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact},
\]
not \(D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\sing}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Nuisance preservation of the live rows]
\label{lem:rank-two-nuisance-preserves-rows}
Assume
\[
  \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
  \qquad
  \omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\exact})=0.
\]
Assume also the live-pair Schur condition
\[
  \lambda_{\min}
  \left(
  [e_7,e_Y]^*
  (I-P_{D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}})
  [e_7,e_Y]
  \right)
  \ge Q^{-A}.
\]
Then
\[
  (\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0,
\]
and the live pair remains two-dimensional and polynomially conditioned modulo
\(D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
The first row-deadness condition gives \(\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0\).
The second gives \(\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\exact})=0\).  Since
\[
  \ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\]
we get \(\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\exact})=0\).  The Schur condition rules out
\[
  e_7\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact},
  \qquad
  e_Y\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact},
\]
and
\[
  e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\exact}.
\]
Thus exact nuisance is both row-dead and live-pair preserving.
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Nuisance-safe contribution to \(B_Y=0\)]
\label{cor:rank-two-nuisance-safe-by-rows}
If the actual-source rows and local completion/transition rows also satisfy
\[
  \ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
  \qquad
  \ell_9^Y(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]
and
\[
  D_{\loc}(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]
then
\[
  B_Y=0.
\]
\end{corollary}

% === audit-rank-two-nuisance-preserves-rows ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Only exact row-dead nuisance enters the denominator.
%   - Coherent nuisance routes to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\).
%   - Residual nuisance routes to \(R_X^{2Y}\).
% Failure modes:
%   - Transverse septic nuisance: \(\Delta_7^\nabla(n)\ne0\).
%   - Primitive \(Y12\) nuisance: \(\omega_Y(P_Wn)\ne0\).
%   - \(e_7\) killed, \(e_Y\) killed, or \(e_Y-\lambda e_7\) in the denominator.
%   - Poor Schur angle.
% Do not use:
%   - Do not quotient coherent/residual live-looking terms as exact nuisance.
%   - Do not use derivative wall components as D4 output.
```

## 2. Replace one stale sentence in the existing D4 area

**Exact place:** in `proof_of_rh(597).tex`, inside

```latex
\begin{remark}[Associated-graded D4 determinant certificate]
\label{rem:associated-graded-d4-determinant-certificate}
...
\end{remark}
```

around line **36776**.

The paper already has the clean triangular D4 carrier matrix and determinant computation nearby, so do **not** add another D4 matrix theorem. Instead, replace any wording that treats \(\beta_9\Gamma_G\) as a residual or suggests a scalar/jet wall with this sentence. The existing draft already recognizes the same-scale \(\beta_9\Gamma_G\) term belongs to the effective leading matrix; preserve and strengthen that point. 

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-d4-determinant-correction ===
% Purpose:
%   Repair stale D4 determinant wording after row-normalization.
% Depends on:
%   Clean triangular D4 carrier matrix.
% Proves:
%   \(\beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G\) belongs to the leading matrix and the D4 wall is plain.
% Status: conditional / replacement text.

In row-normal coordinates the effective clean D4 matrix is
\[
L_{\rm clean}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
\beta_9^{\tri}:=\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7.
\]
The same-scale term \(\beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G\) belongs to the effective leading
matrix, not to the residual.  Thus the determinant condition is
\[
\det L_{\rm clean}^{\tri}=c_7\alpha_9,
\]
and the clean D4 carrier matrix is triangularly invertible on every
coefficient-good patch where \(c_7\) and \(\alpha_9\) are polynomially bounded
above and below.  Consequently the D4 wall produced by this row is the plain
two-component wall
\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]
not a derivative wall involving \(D\Gamma_G\) or \(D^2\Gamma_G\).
```

## 3. Insert wall-variable completion and finite-atlas Riesz

**Exact place:** in `proof_of_rh(597).tex`, insert immediately **after**

```latex
\begin{remark}[Carrier quotient version of the source-weighted residual]
\label{rem:carrier-quotient-source-weighted-residual}
...
\end{remark}
```

and immediately **before**

```latex
\begin{proposition}[Source-divisibility of the established fixed-sector tower]
\label{prop:source-divisibility-established-fixed-sector-tower}
```

This is around line **37794**. This location is best because the paper has just introduced the proof-relevant row-energy object \((H\Gamma_G,H\Gamma_{G,2})\), and the next proposition discusses the vulnerability of the common \(H\) factor. The completion/Riesz package explains how the plain wall becomes a finite-atlas analytic object before discussing source divisibility.

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-wall-variable-completion ===
% Purpose:
%   Complete the honest plain rank-two wall variables by finite-atlas representatives.
% Depends on:
%   \(B_Y=0\), row-normal-form conventions, and coefficient-good D4 routing.
% Proves:
%   \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) are close to finite wall spaces \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{lemma}[Source-curvature completion for the rank-two plain wall]
\label{lem:rank-two-source-curvature-completion}
Let \(I\Subset I^*\) be a chart-good interval with \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer, and set
\[
  p:=q''.
\]
Assume there is a finite source space \(\mathcal P_I\) and \(p_X\in\mathcal P_I\)
such that
\[
  \|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]
Let
\[
  \mathcal V_I:=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
  \qquad
  \mathcal A_1:=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\},
\]
and choose \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) with \(q_X''=p_X\), with affine modes included
or normalized.  Define
\[
  \mathcal W_G
  :=
  \mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]
and
\[
  \mathcal W_{G2}
  :=
  D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
  \operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]
If
\[
  \|q_X\|_{C^4_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{C_0},
\]
then
\[
  G_X:=\Gamma_G(q_X)\in\mathcal W_G,
  \qquad
  G_{2,X}:=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]
and, after increasing \(B_0\),
\[
  \|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
  +
  \|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
  \le Q^{-B}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof sketch]
Use
\[
  \Gamma_G(q)=-6q''+36q-12q^3=-6p+36q-12q^3,
\]
and
\[
  \Gamma_{G,2}(q)=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''=p''-6p+6q^2p.
\]
Since \(q_X''=p_X\), the claimed finite-space inclusions are immediate.

Set \(r=q-q_X\).  Then \(r''=p-p_X\), and affine reconstruction gives
\[
  \|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}
  \le Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}.
\]
The residuals are
\[
  R_G=-6r''+(36-36q_X^2)r-36q_Xr^2-12r^3,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
  R_{G2}
  &=
  r^{(4)}-6r''
  +6q_X^2r''
  +12q_Xr q_X''
  +12q_Xr r''
  +6r^2q_X''
  +6r^2r'' .
\end{aligned}
\]
One-dimensional product estimates yield the stated \(H^2_{\sc}\times L^2\) residual
bound.
\end{proof}

% === audit-rank-two-wall-variable-completion ===
% Guardrails:
%   - This is analytic completion only; it does not create derivative D4 channels.
%   - Complete \(p=q''\) in \(H^2_{\sc}\), not merely in \(L^2\).
%   - Include or normalize affine modes.
%   - \(B_2\), tail, cutoff, and smoothing residuals must be exact, included in \(\mathcal P_I\), or \(H^2_{\sc}\)-small.
% Failure modes:
%   - Only \(L^2/H^1\) source completion.
%   - Uncontrolled affine reconstruction.
%   - Missing \(B_2,DB_2,D^2B_2\) or tail/cutoff derivative ledger.
%   - \(G_X,G_{2,X}\) not in \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\).
% Do not use:
%   - Do not use \(D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G\) as D4 wall components.
```

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-finite-atlas-riesz-2Y-p ===
% Purpose:
%   Supply Markov/Remez finite-atlas control for the honest plain rank-two wall.
% Depends on:
%   WallVariableCompletion_{2Y}, the \(p=q''\) source atlas, and product-Riesz.
% Proves:
%   LocalMarkov_{\Gamma_G} and vector Remez for \(W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\).
% Status: finite-audit.

\begin{theorem}[FiniteAtlasRiesz in \(p=q''\) form]
\label{thm:finite-atlas-riesz-2Y-p}
Assume on each chart-good \(I\Subset I^*\):
\begin{enumerate}
\item \(\dim\mathcal P_I\le R^C\) and \(\mathcal P_I\) has a \(Q^C\)-Riesz basis in \(L^2(I^*)\).
\item
\[
  \|D^2p_X\|_{L^2(I)}\le Q^C\|p_X\|_{L^2(I^*)},
  \qquad p_X\in\mathcal P_I.
\]
\item Affine reconstruction into \(\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I\) is polynomially controlled.
\item \(\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I\) and
\(\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I\) have \(Q^C\)-conditioned quotient bases and bounded leverage.
\item All relevant chart denominators are \(Q^{-C}\)-bounded below on \(I^*\).
\item For every predetermined \(E\subset I\) with \(|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|\), Remez holds for
\[
  \mathcal W_G
  =
  \mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I
\]
and
\[
  \mathcal W_{G2}
  =
  D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
  \operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]
\end{enumerate}
Then
\[
  \|w\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
  \le Q^C\|w\|_{L^2(I^*)},
  \qquad w\in\mathcal W_G.
\]
Moreover, after \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\),
\[
  |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \int_E W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2-Q^{-B},
\]
where
\[
  W_2:=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]
\end{theorem}

% === audit-rank-two-finite-atlas-riesz-2Y-p ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Apply this only to finite representatives supplied by WallVariableCompletion.
%   - Product spaces must be quotient-conditioned; raw product monomial independence is not assumed.
%   - Remez sets must be predetermined and \(R^{-C}\)-large.
% Failure modes:
%   - No \(D^2\mathcal P_I\) control.
%   - Affine reconstruction instability.
%   - Product leverage spike.
%   - Denominator singularity.
%   - No \(R^{-C}\)-Remez.
% Do not use:
%   - Do not use the jet wall as D4/O2O3 wall.
```

## 4. Insert core transfer and O2/O3 interface

**Exact place:** in `proof_of_rh(597).tex`, insert immediately **after**

```latex
\begin{remark}[Interpretation of the source-collapse countermodel]
\label{rem:interpretation-source-collapse-countermodel}
...
\end{remark}
```

and before

```latex
\begin{remark}[Interval-local source collapse]
\label{rem:interval-local-source-collapse}
```

This is around line **37980**. The reason is structural: the source-collapse countermodel says local rank-two algebra alone cannot force source-weighted mass; the core transfer block is explicitly conditional on having unweighted wall mass and coefficient-good cores, so it belongs right after the countermodel interpretation and before the longer source-collapse heuristics.

```latex
% === wip-rank-two-core-transfer-o2o3 ===
% Purpose:
%   Transfer unweighted plain wall mass to weighted scalar O2/O3 mass using predetermined cores.
% Depends on:
%   \(B_Y=0\), row-normal D4, FiniteAtlasRiesz vector Remez, RankTwoCoercivity, and Gate--1.
% Proves:
%   A scalar \(G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\) gives bounded-charge O2/O3 input.
% Status: finite-audit / conditional.

\begin{definition}[D4 coefficient-good core]
\label{def:d4-coeff-core-2Y}
For each routed block \(I\), choose before wall extraction a core
\[
  E_I^{\core}\subset I
\]
depending only on
\[
  H,\ c_7,\ \alpha_9,\ \beta_9^{\tri},\ N_{\sm},
  \quad\text{and chart data,}
\]
not on
\[
  \Gamma_G,\ \Gamma_{G,2},\ W_2,\ \text{hard atoms, or live residuals}.
\]
The core is admissible if
\[
  |E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]
and on \(E_I^{\core}\),
\[
  |H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]
\[
  |c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9^{\tri}|
  \le Q^A,
\]
and
\[
  N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Core weighted plain-wall transfer]
\label{lem:core-weighted-plain-wall-transfer-2Y}
Assume admissible cores and vector Remez:
\[
  |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \int_E W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2\,d\nu.
\]
Then
\[
  \int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu
  \ge Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2\,d\nu.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Scalar component selection]
\label{lem:rank-two-scalar-component-selection}
If
\[
  \int N_{\sm}|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu\ge Q^{-A},
\]
then there exists a fixed component
\[
  G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]
chosen at the smooth aggregate stage such that
\[
  \int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[Rank-two wall degree]
\label{def:rank-two-wall-degree}
For
\[
  G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]
define
\[
  d_0(G_\star):=d_N+2d_H+2d_{G_\star}.
\]
Set
\[
  d_{\max}^{(2)}
  :=
  d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
\]
Conservatively,
\[
  d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\le 3d_q,
\]
so
\[
  d_{\max}^{(2)}\le d_N+2d_H+6d_q.
\]
If \(H\) is smooth-renormalizable and carries no branch charge, set \(d_H=0\).
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Scalar O2/O3 interface for rank two]
\label{thm:rank-two-scalar-o2o3-interface}
Assume weighted rank-two plain wall mass has been transferred, and assume
\[
  \mathbb C_{d_{\max}^{(2)}}[y]\subset V_{\tr}.
\]
Assume the scalar O2/O3 near-pure separation holds uniformly for every fixed \(d\le d_{\max}^{(2)}\):
\[
  \operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\mu_{\sub})}
  \left(
  1,
  \operatorname{span}\{R_{\fold},R_{\fold}^2,\ldots,R_{\fold}^{d}\}
  \right)^2
  \ge Q^{-A(d_{\max}^{(2)})}.
\]
Then the selected scalar wall
\[
  W_\star=N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2
\]
is a valid bounded-charge O2/O3 input.
\end{theorem}

% === audit-rank-two-core-transfer-o2o3 ===
% Guardrails:
%   - Cores are chosen before wall extraction.
%   - \(G_\star\) is selected at smooth aggregate level, not atomwise.
%   - Use \(\beta_9^{\tri}\), not raw \(\beta_9\).
%   - O2/O3 receives only \(G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\).
% Failure modes:
%   - No \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined core.
%   - \(H\), \(c_7\), \(\alpha_9\), or \(\beta_9^{\tri}\) degenerates.
%   - \(N_{\sm}\) cuts away the core.
%   - Vector Remez fails.
%   - \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\) is not \(O(1)\).
%   - Near-pure moment separation fails at \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\).
% Do not use:
%   - Do not hard-code \(d_{\max}=14\) unless the audit proves \(d_{\max}^{(2)}\le14\).
%   - Do not use derivative wall components as D4/O2/O3 inputs.
%   - Do not project coherent amplitude before O2/O3 unless branch complexity is controlled.
```

# Summary of changes I made while rewriting

I made five improvements relative to the raw agent blocks.

First, I removed duplicated row-normal definitions from every module and centralized them in the first insertion.

Second, I changed all “proved” language to **finite-audit / conditional** where the agents had not supplied actual matrix entries.

Third, I converted the wall-completion block into a clean \(p=q''\) source-completion statement, avoiding the stronger and less natural primitive \(H^4(q)\) framing.

Fourth, I kept the D4 wall strictly plain:
\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]
No jet wall is inserted anywhere.

Fifth, I moved core transfer after the source-collapse countermodel interpretation, so it reads as a conditional transfer mechanism rather than as a claim that local rank-two algebra already defeats source collapse.

# Edit 1 — replace the old Gate–2 derivative-complexity package with a no-go/fallback remark

**Where:** In `\section{Downstream local closure package: Gate 2, wall escape, and carrier rows}` / `\label{sec:downstream-local-closure-package}`.

```latex
\begin{remark}[Status of the following derivative-complexity package]
\label{rem:status-derivative-complexity-package}
```

through the end of

```latex
\begin{proposition}[Conditional Gate--2 transfer from the analytic-good-patch package]
\label{prop:conditional-gate2-transfer-good-patch}
...
\end{proposition}
```

**Replace with:**

```latex
\begin{remark}[Historical derivative-complexity package is fallback]
\label{rem:status-derivative-complexity-package}
The finite-order derivative-complexity package formerly used to propagate
pointwise or thick-set smallness is no longer the active ordinary Gate--2
mechanism.

The active ordinary branch is
\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate 1}
+
|u'|_\infty\text{ source thickening}
+
L^2\text{-input real rigidity}
\Longrightarrow
\text{Gate 2},
}
\]
as stated in Theorem~\ref{thm:gate-two-from-gate-one-l2-rigidity}.

Accordingly, the old derivative-complexity hypotheses should be read only as
historical/fallback sufficient conditions.  They are not an independent active
gate, and they should not be cited as a required \(D1/D2\)-style downstream
input in the ordinary real-canonical branch.

If a later argument chooses to work outside the \(L^2\)-input rigidity route,
then it must explicitly reintroduce a derivative-complexity hypothesis and
state the associated propagation theorem.  Otherwise, all references to the
old \(D1/D2\) package are no-go/fallback bookkeeping.
\end{remark}
```

**Why:** This removes the lengthy historical propagation package while preserving its status. The current active Gate–2 theorem is already `\ref{thm:gate-two-from-gate-one-l2-rigidity}`. The draft itself says the old \(D1/D2\) status should be historical/fallback, so this replacement is consistent with the current paper.

```latex
\begin{remark}[Retired and quarantined gate routes]
\label{rem:retired-quarantined-gate-routes}
The current architecture uses the following active/fallback distinction.

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
\text{object} & \text{current status} & \text{reason}\\
\hline
\text{Gate--1} &
\text{active, upstream arithmetic input} &
\text{requires actual-zeta source nonrecurrence, not local geometry}\\
\text{Gate--2} &
\text{downstream consequence of Gate--1 in the ordinary branch} &
\text{supplied by source thickening and }L^2\text{-input rigidity}\\
D1/D2 &
\text{retired/fallback} &
\text{old Remez/pointwise derivative propagation is no longer active}\\
D3 &
\text{retired/fallback} &
\text{old source-robust wall row is replaced by gauge-locked wall escape}\\
D4 &
\text{active only as carrier/quotient row conversion} &
\text{scalar }I\text{-coordinate D4 is quarantined}\\
\text{rank-one }Y12\text{ moving wall} &
\text{fallback} &
\text{rank two bypasses }\rho_\Gamma\text{ and the forced-ODE trap if }B_Y=0\\
\text{rank-two D4 plain wall} &
\text{active conditional route} &
\text{requires row-normal exactness and coefficient/core routing}
\end{array}
\]

Thus the old broad downstream chain
\[
D1+D2+D3+D4
\]
is no longer the operative proof stack.  The active ordinary downstream stack is
\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate 1}
+
|u'|_\infty\text{ source thickening}
+
L^2\text{-input real rigidity}
\Longrightarrow
\text{Gate 2},
}
\]
followed by
\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate 2}
+
\text{gauge-locked wall escape}
+
\text{carrier/quotient D4}
+
\text{row mobility}
\Longrightarrow
\text{row energy}.
}
\]

For the current rank-two route, the D4 output is the plain wall
\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]
after row-normalization and coefficient-good routing.  It is not a jet wall
involving \(D\Gamma_G\) or \(D^2\Gamma_G\) unless a separate derivative-D4
channel theorem is proved.
\end{remark}
```

**Why:** This gives one place to cite when deleting or condensing older D1/D2/D3/D4 discussion.

**Replace with:**

```latex
\subsection{Ordinary packet-frequency Gate--1 inputs}
\label{subsec:ordinary-packet-frequency-wronskian-route}

Work in the ordinary packet-frequency regime
\[
\lambda Q\asymp1,
\qquad
R\asymp\frac{\log Q}{\log\log Q},
\qquad
\mathcal D=\lambda\partial_m.
\]
The proof-level source object is the full canonical curvature
\[
q''_{\can}=B_2+P_2+D_2+E_2.
\]
The ordinary Wronskian route is retained only as a named-input package:
\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{O1}+\mathrm{O2}+\mathrm{O3}
\Longrightarrow
\text{ordinary Gate--1}.
}
\]
None of \(\mathrm{O1}\), \(\mathrm{O2}\), or \(\mathrm{O3}\) is proved in this
draft.

\begin{definition}[O1: fixed-width cell nonrecurrence]
\label{def:O1-fixed-width-cell-nonrecurrence}
For each admissible ordinary-scale zero \(Z_\alpha(m_0)=0\), let \(B_r\) denote
the full canonical fixed-width packet cell vector, including main branch,
folded dual branch, gamma/background, and error contributions in the selected
AFE convention.  The O1 input is
\[
\boxed{
\sum_r |B_r(m_0,\alpha)|^2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]
This is a pointwise actual-orbit nonrecurrence statement at specified
\(Z_\alpha\)-zeros.  It is not a consequence of diagonal mass, local cell
geometry, or phase-relaxed coefficient magnitudes.
\end{definition}

\begin{hypothesis}[O1a: same-branch actual-orbit cell mass]
\label{hyp:O1a-same-branch-cell-mass}
The same-branch part of O1 has polynomial mass:
\[
\boxed{
\|B^+(m_0,\alpha)\|_2^2+\|B^-(m_0,\alpha)\|_2^2
\ge Q^{-A}
}
\]
for every admissible ordinary-scale zero \(Z_\alpha(m_0)=0\), in the canonical
packet-frequency regime.
\end{hypothesis}

\begin{hypothesis}[O1b: phase-locked folded anti-alignment exclusion]
\label{hyp:O1b-phase-locked-folded-anti-alignment}
The main and folded-dual cell vectors cannot cancel to polynomial accuracy
after the actual \(Z_\alpha\)-locked phase is imposed:
\[
\boxed{
\|B^+ + e^{i\Theta_Z(m_0,\alpha)}B^- + B^{\rm bg}+B^E\|_2^2
\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]
This is an actual-orbit phase-locking input, not a torus-relaxed statement.
\end{hypothesis}

\begin{definition}[O2: cofactor-vector nonrecurrence]
\label{def:O2-cofactor-vector-nonrecurrence}
Let \(\mathbf M(m_0,\alpha)\) be the cofactor/Hodge-dual vector of the canonical
\(q''_{\can}\)-jet block at the ordinary zero \(Z_\alpha(m_0)=0\).  The O2 input is
\[
\boxed{
|\mathbf M(m_0,\alpha)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]
Averaged exterior mass or large-sieve estimates do not imply O2 without an
additional bad-zero exclusion theorem.
\end{definition}

\begin{hypothesis}[O2-BZ: \(Z\)--cofactor bad-zero exclusion]
\label{hyp:O2-BZ-z-cofactor-bad-zero-exclusion}
At every admissible ordinary-scale zero \(Z_\alpha(m_0)=0\), the cofactor vector
avoids the bad-zero locus:
\[
\boxed{
|\mathbf M(m_0,\alpha)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]
\end{hypothesis}

\begin{lemma}[O2 from bad-zero exclusion]
\label{lem:O2-from-BZ}
Hypothesis~\ref{hyp:O2-BZ-z-cofactor-bad-zero-exclusion} implies
Definition~\ref{def:O2-cofactor-vector-nonrecurrence}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}[O3: Wronskian angle nonalignment / ordinary incidence]
\label{def:O3-wronskian-angle-nonalignment}
Let \(\mathbf z'(m_0,\alpha)\) be the \(Z_\alpha\)-row derivative vector at an
ordinary-scale zero, and let \(\mathbf M(m_0,\alpha)\) be the canonical cofactor
vector.  The O3 input is the pointwise angle lower bound
\[
\boxed{
|\langle \mathbf z'(m_0,\alpha),\mathbf M(m_0,\alpha)\rangle|
\ge Q^{-A}|\mathbf z'(m_0,\alpha)|\,|\mathbf M(m_0,\alpha)|.
}
\]
O3 is an actual prime-log incidence theorem.  It is not implied by O1 and O2.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}[Ordinary Gate--1 from O1--O3]
\label{thm:ordinary-gate-one-from-O1-O2-O3}
Assume O1, O2, and O3 for every admissible ordinary-scale zero.  Then the
ordinary packet-frequency branch satisfies Gate--1 for the full canonical source
\(q''_{\can}=B_2+P_2+D_2+E_2\):
\[
\boxed{
\int_I |q''_{\can}(m)|^2\,dm\ge Q^{-A}|I|
}
\]
on the corresponding ordinary packet intervals, after the stated canonical
transfer and derivative-budget hypotheses.
\end{theorem}

\begin{remark}[Ordinary-route no-go summary]
\label{rem:ordinary-route-no-go-summary}
The following implications are not available:
\[
\text{diagonal mass}\not\Rightarrow\mathrm{O1},
\qquad
\mathrm{O1}+\mathrm{O2}\not\Rightarrow\mathrm{O3},
\]
\[
\text{generic determinant nonvanishing}\not\Rightarrow\mathrm{O3},
\qquad
\text{averaged large sieve}\not\Rightarrow\mathrm{O2}.
\]
Low-prime or proxy blocks are diagnostic only.  Proof-level ordinary Gate--1
requires the full canonical source \(q''_{\can}=B_2+P_2+D_2+E_2\).
\end{remark}
```

**Replace with:**

```latex
\subsection{Singular and transition Gate--1 branches}
\label{subsec:singular-transition-gate-one-branches}

The ordinary O1--O3 route covers only the packet-frequency regime
\[
\lambda Q\asymp1.
\]
Singular and transition packets require separate named inputs.

\begin{definition}[S1: singular explicit-formula anti-design exclusion]
\label{def:S1-singular-explicit-formula-anti-design}
In the deep singular regime
\[
\lambda Q\ll(\log Q)^{-C},
\]
the S1 input is the explicit-formula anti-design lower bound
\[
\boxed{
|\mathbf P_R-\mathbf G_R-\mathbf T_R|_2\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]
This is a singular actual-zeta arithmetic input.  It is not a local singular
geometry theorem and not a refinement of the ordinary O1--O3 route.
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[S1 is not local singular geometry]
\label{rem:S1-not-local-singular-geometry}
The deep singular regime sees long-prime explicit-formula information.  Local
band-limited singular geometry or finite same-scale packet structure does not
prove S1.
\end{remark}

\begin{definition}[T1: weighted transition source observability]
\label{def:T1-weighted-transition-source-observability}
In the transition regime
\[
(\log Q)^{-C}\lesssim\lambda Q\lesssim1,
\]
the T1 input is a polynomial-loss transfer of source-curvature observability
from either the ordinary or singular model into the transition chart:
\[
\boxed{
\int_{I_{\rm tr}} |q''_{\can}(m)|^2\,d\mu_{\rm tr}(m)
\ge Q^{-A}\mu_{\rm tr}(I_{\rm tr}).
}
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Weighted transition chart loss]
\label{lem:weighted-transition-chart-loss}
Assume T1 and polynomially controlled transition chart weights.  Then the
transition branch supplies the Gate--1 source lower bound with only polynomial
loss.
\end{lemma}

\begin{remark}[Ordinary, transition, and singular routing]
\label{rem:ordinary-transition-singular-routing}
The complete Gate--1 input is the disjunction
\[
\boxed{
\text{ordinary branch: O1+O2+O3,}
}
\]
\[
\boxed{
\text{transition branch: T1,}
}
\]
\[
\boxed{
\text{deep singular branch: S1.}
}
\]
A proof cannot force the deep singular branch through the ordinary
packet-frequency Wronskian, and it cannot treat transition packets without a
polynomial transition-loss theorem.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Deep singular Gate--1 alternative]
\label{thm:deep-singular-gate-one-alternative}
Assume S1 in the deep singular regime, with the canonical gamma/background and
tail terms included in the same convention as \(q''_{\can}\).  Then the deep
singular branch supplies the Gate--1 source lower bound with polynomial loss.
\end{theorem}
```

with:

```latex
\begin{remark}[Terminal conditional dependency ledger]
\label{rem:terminal-conditional-dependency-ledger}
The present draft is a conditional architecture, not a closed proof of \(\RH\).

The active upstream arithmetic inputs are:
\[
\boxed{\mathrm{O1}:\ \text{fixed-width cell nonrecurrence}},
\]
\[
\boxed{\mathrm{O2}:\ \text{cofactor-vector nonrecurrence}},
\]
\[
\boxed{\mathrm{O3}:\ \text{Wronskian angle nonalignment}},
\]
\[
\boxed{\mathrm{S1}:\ \text{singular explicit-formula anti-design exclusion}},
\]
and
\[
\boxed{\mathrm{T1}:\ \text{weighted transition source observability}}.
\]

The active ordinary downstream route is:
\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate 1}
+
|u'|_\infty\text{ source thickening}
+
L^2\text{-input real rigidity}
\Longrightarrow
\text{Gate 2}.
}
\]
The active row-output route is:
\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate 2}
+
\text{gauge-locked wall escape}
+
\text{carrier/quotient D4}
+
\text{row mobility}
\Longrightarrow
\text{row energy}.
}
\]

For the current rank-two D4 branch, the additional finite-audit modules are:
\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TwoRowExactSequence}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}
\Longrightarrow B_Y=0,
}
\]
\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
\Longrightarrow
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
}
\]
\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{SourceCurvatureCompletion}^{(2)}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}^{p}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\Longrightarrow
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A},
}
\]
where
\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]
and
\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{ScalarBoundedChargeO2O3}(d_{\max}^{(2)})
}
\]
supplies the scalar O2/O3 input after selecting
\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]
at the smooth aggregate stage.

The old \(D1/D2/D3\) routes and scalar \(I\)-coordinate D4 are historical or
fallback.  The active D4 statement is carrier/quotient row conversion for the
canonical scalar source \(u=q''_{\can}\).  It is not scalar \(I\)-coordinate
coercivity, and it does not provide derivative wall components unless a
separate derivative-D4 channel theorem is proved.
\end{remark}
```

# What this accomplishes

These edits remove or condense the stale parts as follows.

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{old material} & \text{new status}\\
\hline
D1/D2\text{ derivative propagation} & \text{historical/fallback no-go remark}\\
D3\text{ source-robust wall row} & \text{replaced by gauge-locked wall escape + D4}\\
D4\text{ scalar }I\text{-coordinate} & \text{fallback/quarantined}\\
O1/O2/O3\text{ long derivations} & \text{compact named-input registry}\\
ordinary no-go archive & \text{centralized in }\ref{sec:no-go-and-audit-ledger}\\
Gate2\text{ as independent input} & \text{downstream consequence of Gate1 in ordinary branch}\\
rank-two wall & \text{plain wall only }H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\end{array}
\]

I would make these edits before adding more rank-two material; otherwise the draft will continue to carry two incompatible architectures in parallel.
